title: An Ordinary Village Morning
body: Morning smoke hangs low over the village. Your hands still ache from yesterday's chores, and the day ahead promises nothing grand: water to carry, tools to mend, herbs to sort, and a body that will stay weak unless you make it useful. Before the road can become a story, the village is still your whole world: mud, iron, bitter medicine, shrine ash, and familiar voices.

choices.label: Walk through the village square and greet the people already awake
Check in with family before the day begins
Carry stones at the river before chores
Help Blacksmith Guo mend farm tools

recordNpcConversation.topic: Helped him mend farm tools and learned that bad iron complains before it fails.

choices.label: Sort bitter herbs for Aunt Lin

recordNpcConversation.topic: Sorted bitter herbs for her and learned that medicine and poison can look nearly identical.

title: Needle Thread And Old Cloth
body:
Home has its own kind of training: carrying water without spilling, listening without arguing, and mending what the day will tear apart. Before you leave, your family presses a small sewing roll into your hand. It is plain cloth, bone needles, waxed thread, and a few folded patches.

"Roads are hard on sleeves, sandals, and pride," they say. "Fix the first two before the third gets expensive."

choices.label: Head to the village square with the sewing roll tucked away
Return to the morning chores

title: The Village Wakes
body:
The square wakes in layers. Old Ren sits with his cane across his knees, pretending not to watch every stance in sight. Aunt Lin ties bundles of herbs with red cord. Blacksmith Guo carries a repaired hinge still warm from the forge. Shrine Keeper Mei sweeps ash into a line so neat it feels like a lesson.

You slow near the well, listening as small rumors move from mouth to mouth: a broken wagon, frightened traders, scorch marks on the south road.

"Everyone keeps looking south," you say. "What happened out there?"

choices.label: Ask Old Ren why the caravan guards look nervous

recordNpcConversation.topic: Warned you that the south road feels wrong: frightened guards, bad orders, and danger in the hills.

choices.label: Ask Aunt Lin what medicine the traders requested

recordNpcConversation.topic: Said the traders needed burn salve and pain medicine, and that clean round burns are not bandit work.

choices.label: Ask Blacksmith Guo about the broken wagon axle

recordNpcConversation.topic: Showed you axle iron burned too cleanly for ordinary damage.

choices.label: Ask Shrine Keeper Mei what the incense smoke warned her about

recordNpcConversation.topic: Told you the shrine bell rang by itself in the night, thin as thread from the south.

choices.label: Take the gathered rumors toward the south road
Return home before choosing where to help

title: Old Ren Watches The Road
body:
You stop beside Old Ren's stool and nod toward the nervous caravan guards.

"You used to walk those roads. Are they afraid of bandits?"

Old Ren taps his cane against the packed dirt.

"Caravan guards get nervous for three reasons: bad pay, bad orders, or something in the hills that makes bad men act like prey. The south road has all three smells today."

"So I should stay away?"

"No. If you go, keep your ribs guarded and listen before you swing."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Old Ren's warning

title: Aunt Lin's Bitter Warning
body:
You carry a bundle of bitterleaf to Aunt Lin's table and lower your voice.

"The traders came to you first. What did they need?"

Aunt Lin does not look up from her herbs.

"Fever powder, burn salve, and something to keep a child quiet through pain."

"That sounds worse than a broken wagon."

She cuts a root too sharply.

"Bandits make bruises. Clean round burns are not bandit work."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Aunt Lin's warning

title: Guo's Broken Axle
body:
You find Guo beside the forge, turning a sliver of axle iron in his fingers.

"Can you fix it?"

He shows you the break: too smooth, too hot, as if a tiny sun passed through the metal and kept walking.

"I can fix a wheel. I cannot fix whatever made this."

"Then what does the road need?"

He closes his fist around the iron.

"More than a repaired wheel."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Guo's warning

title: Mei Counts The Smoke
body:
You pause at the shrine while Mei watches incense drift sideways though the morning air is still.

"Is that a bad sign?"

"A sign is only a sign. Fear makes it larger. Pride makes it useless."

"And what do you make of it?"

"The shrine bell rang once in the night without a hand touching it. The sound came from the south, thin as thread. Trouble is walking toward the immortal world, and mortals are already in its footprints."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Mei's warning

title: Cold Stones
body: The river does not care about ambition. It numbs your calves, steals your breath, and makes every stone heavier than it looked from shore. By the time you stagger back, your arms shake, but your feet remember how not to slip.

choices.label: Return to the village square and hear what people are saying

title: Honest Iron
body:
Guo gives you a cracked hoe, not a sword.

"I was hoping for something sharper," you say.

"Then learn not to ruin dull things first."

You pump the bellows, hold the tongs, and learn that bad iron complains before it fails. When a boy runs past the forge shouting about a broken wagon on the south road, Guo's hammer stops mid-swing.

choices.label: Return to the village square and ask what happened

title: Leaves That Look Alike
body:
Aunt Lin makes you sort roots until your fingertips smell bitter.

"These two look the same," you complain.

"Then look again. Most people die because they cannot tell medicine from poison until too late."

You swallow the answer you wanted to give her. Then a wounded mule limps into view, dragging half a harness and panic behind it.

choices.label: Return to the village square with the medicine satchel

title: Trouble On The South Road
body: At first it sounds like a normal road accident: a snapped axle, frightened traders, someone crying for help. Then you see the scorch mark on the dirt. It is too clean, too round, and still smoking without flame.

choices.label: Step into the broken trade road

title: Ten Days Before The Mountain Gate
body: The Azure Cloud Sect will open its mountain gate in ten days. Yesterday, immortals were only rumor. Now the road has left proof in your hands: jade, blood, cloud-iron, whispers, or a black-thread token. If you mean to survive the chance that found you, your mortal foundation has to become worth something quickly.

choices.label: Train your body at the river stone ford
Practice mortal martial arts in the abandoned threshing yard
Study herbs and crude alchemy with the village herbalist
Work the village forge and prepare road gear
Spend a quiet day with family, shrine, and road preparations
Seek guidance from the village teachers
Leave for the Azure Cloud mountain gate now
Ask whether your current qi is enough for the sect exam

title: Not Enough Qi
body:
The token warms when you think of the mountain, but your body answers with only a thin, uncertain heat. Mei watches your breathing. Aunt Lin watches your hands. Old Ren watches your stance.

"You can still go," Mei says, "but do not mistake being chosen for being ready. The sect mountain is rich with qi. If your body has not been tempered enough to endure it, the first trial may do the tempering for you. Painfully."

Aunt Lin snorts.

"Get more rough qi in your bones before you run toward immortals."

choices.label: Return to preparation

title: Village Teachers
body: The village has no immortals, but it has people who know what breaks mortals. Old Ren watches the practice yard with a cane across his knees. Aunt Lin sorts bitter roots in the shade. Blacksmith Guo hammers iron by sound. Shrine Keeper Mei sweeps ash from a cracked altar and counts every breath.

choices.label: Ask Old Ren to judge your martial stance

recordNpcConversation.topic: Asked him to judge your martial stance before the sect exam.

choices.label: Help Aunt Lin prepare exam medicine

recordNpcConversation.topic: Asked to help prepare medicine for the sect exam.

choices.label: Ask Blacksmith Guo for an honest forge lesson

recordNpcConversation.topic: Asked him for a forge lesson and a practical road preparation.

choices.label: Ask Shrine Keeper Mei to teach you breath counting

recordNpcConversation.topic: Asked her to teach breath counting before the mountain trial.

choices.label: Step back before the trouble swallows you

title: Old Ren's Cane
body:
Old Ren was only a caravan guard, not a cultivator, but half the village still walks around his yard when he is holding a cane. He looks at your feet first, then your shoulders, then the space your ribs forgot to protect.

"Before you copy a fist form, learn why a body falls. Immortals call their power qi. Mortals meet it first as pressure, pain, heat, and breath. Temper the body badly, and qi will only find better places to break you."

choices.label: Hold your stance while Old Ren tries to break it
Return to the village teachers

title: Correction By Cane
body:
Old Ren's cane taps your ankle, hip, ribs, and wrist. Each touch is humiliatingly small. Each one fixes a leak in your strength. When he finally swings for real, your body answers before your pride can flinch.

"That warmth in your bones is not cultivation," he says. "It is your flesh learning not to waste what passes through it."

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Ren's Foundation Drills
body:
Old Ren breaks your stance with one bored push. Then he makes you rebuild it from the feet up: heel, knee, hip, breath, guard. No glory. Just the parts you skipped.

"Do not chase qi," he says. "Let the body become something qi cannot immediately ruin."

choices.label: Return after more practice
Return to the village square

title: Aunt Lin's Bitter Table
body: Aunt Lin does not ask whether you want to become immortal. She asks whether you can tell fever leaf from throatbane after both have dried brown. Her table is covered in roots, powders, and consequences.

choices.label: Prepare a stable fever powder under Aunt Lin's eye
Return to the village teachers

title: Medicine That Leaves Less Debt
body:
The powder settles pale and even. Aunt Lin sniffs it, says nothing for a very long moment, then slides a crude qi elixir toward you.

"Good medicine is what helps without asking too much back."

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Smoke And Scolding
body: The batch smokes, then clumps, then earns a sound from Aunt Lin that is somehow worse than shouting. She makes you start again with easier herbs and explains exactly how impatience becomes poison.

choices.label: Return after studying herbs and heat
Return to the village square

title: Guo's Honest Iron
body:
Blacksmith Guo listens to metal more than people. He hands you a cracked sickle.

"Immortal swords can wait until you stop ruining mortal tools."

choices.label: Temper the cracked sickle without hiding the flaw
Return to the village teachers

title: The Ring Of Good Iron
body: The sickle rings dull but steady when Guo tests it. He nods once, which from him is nearly applause, and shows you how the same care can bind iron around a walking staff.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: A Crack Made Worse
body: You heat the sickle too eagerly. The crack widens with a tiny, accusing sound. Guo takes it back, breaks it clean, and makes you study the grain of the failure.

choices.label: Return after learning the forge rhythm
Return to the village square

title: Mei's Three Breaths
body:
Shrine Keeper Mei is younger than the title sounds and older than her face suggests. She sweeps ash into a neat line.

"The first immortal test is usually whether a mortal can stop performing long enough to breathe."

choices.label: Match three breaths to the incense smoke
Return to the village teachers

title: Smoke Without Trembling
body: The incense smoke rises straight. Your breath follows without chasing it. Mei smiles like someone watching a door unlock from the inside.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Smoke That Shivers
body:
Your breath keeps trying to impress the shrine instead of match it. Mei lets you fail three times before speaking.

"Better here than on the mountain steps."

choices.label: Return after steadying your breath
Return to the village square

title: River Stone Ford
body: The village river has no spiritual pressure, no immortal formation, and no mercy. It is winter-cold around the knees and strong enough to drag a careless body downstream. The older youths trained here before they failed the sect exam. Their footprints are gone, but the stones remain.

choices.label: Carry river stones against the current
Hold horse stance beneath the little falls
Return to the village square

title: Cold Stone, Mortal Bone
body: You carry stones until your arms tremble and the current begins to feel like an opponent. Nothing mystical answers you. That is the point. Your body learns to continue without applause.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Sinews Like Wet Rope
body: Halfway across the ford your legs almost fold, then something changes. Your breath drops lower, your tendons stop screaming separately, and the river becomes one pressure instead of a hundred little pains.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Under The Little Falls
body: The falling water hammers your shoulders flat and steals heat from your skin. You stay in stance long enough for anger, fear, and ambition to become only breath.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Abandoned Threshing Yard
body: Dry grass grows through cracks in the old threshing yard. Children use it for dares, farmers use it to cut across the fields, and you use it because no one important comes here to laugh at mortal punches.

choices.label: Drill the village fist forms until dusk
Spar with the miller's sons
Practice staff basics with a bamboo pole
Return to the village square

title: Plain Fists
body: The forms are not beautiful. Step, guard, turn the hip, strike. Again. Again. By dusk the motions have less hesitation in them, and your knuckles remember the order better than your thoughts do.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Bruises Given And Taken
body: The miller's sons come at you laughing, then stop laughing when your guard holds. You still take hits, but you answer them. Mortal courage is not enough for immortals, perhaps, but it is not nothing.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: A Lesson In Bruises
body: You learn quickly that knowing a stance and keeping it while someone swings at your face are different skills. The yard sends you home sore, embarrassed, and slightly wiser.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Bamboo Distance
body: The bamboo pole flexes with every strike. You learn the comfort of distance: how a simple staff can make a stronger opponent spend one more breath reaching you.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Village Herbalist's Hut
body: The herbalist's hut smells of bitter roots, drying leaves, and smoke trapped in the rafters. There are no true immortal pills here, only mortal remedies, secondhand warnings, and a clay stove that cracks if treated like a sect furnace.

choices.label: Sort drying herbs by scent and vein
Boil a bitter road tonic without scorching it
Attempt a crude qi elixir with mortal tools
Return to the village square

title: Leaves That Look Alike
body: By afternoon your fingers are stained green and the herbalist has corrected you six times. The seventh time, you catch the mistake before she does. Her grunt of approval is almost a blessing.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Bitter Strength
body: The tonic tastes like boiled bark and regret, but it steadies your hands and warms your stomach. Mortal medicine will not open meridians, but it can keep a mortal body from failing early.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: A Clearer Crude Elixir
body:
The elixir is still crude by any sect standard, but the smoke thins at the right moment and the liquid settles without a sour edge. When a drop touches your tongue, heat moves under your skin instead of into your thoughts.

Aunt Lin watches your hands stop trembling.

"Do not call that cultivation. You are not gathering qi. You are letting a little of it temper the body before the sect teaches you how to breathe it properly."

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Smoke And Ambition
body:
The clay stove spits smoke, the herbs darken too quickly, and the finished elixir smells sharper than it should. When you taste it, heat claws through your muscles before settling into a rough, useful ache.

"That is why mortals should be careful with borrowed qi," Aunt Lin says. "It can temper a body. It can also leave dirt behind."

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Village Forge Yard
body: The blacksmith cannot make flying swords. He can make hinges, ploughshares, nails, knives, and a staff that will not split on the first bad stone. The forge is mortal fire, but mortal fire still teaches discipline.

choices.label: Pump the bellows until your arms shake
Learn to read flaws in scrap iron
Reinforce a walking staff with iron bands
Return to the village square

title: Breath For The Forge
body: The bellows breathe through your arms. Too fast and the fire snaps wild; too slow and it sulks. By evening your shoulders ache, but you understand rhythm in a way lectures never taught.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Fault Lines
body: The blacksmith shows you where iron lies: dull color, wrong ring, a hairline crack waiting for strain. You begin to wonder how many people carry the same signs before they break.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Iron-Banded Staff
body:
The staff remains plain wood, but iron bands now guard the places most likely to split. The blacksmith tests it against the anvil and nods once.

"Do not trust anything that looks too fine for honest use."

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Family Courtyard
body: The courtyard is swept clean, though dust keeps finding its way back. Your family understands rain, debt, harvest, sickness, and hunger. They do not understand sects, except that people leave for them and return changed, if they return.

choices.label: Share a last ordinary meal and listen instead of boasting
Burn incense at the village shrine
Pack for the road and sleep early
Return to the village square

title: Last Ordinary Meal
body: The meal is simple enough to hurt: rice stretched with greens, pickled roots, tea poured twice from the same leaves. No one says that immortality begins by leaving mortal hands behind.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Smoke At The Shrine
body: The shrine statue is cracked, rain-worn, and probably nothing like a true immortal. Still, the incense smoke rises straight for a moment, and your breathing follows it.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: A Mortal Pack
body: You pack cloth wraps, dried grain, a needle, spare cord, and more humility than you expected. None of it looks heroic. All of it may matter before the mountain is done with you.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: The Broken Trade Road
body:
The south road should smell of mule sweat, tea bundles, and dust. Today it smells of split axles, cold ash, and fear. Merchants whisper about bandits, but their eyes keep sliding toward the hills as if the real danger is not standing on the road at all. A black thread is tied around one broken wagon spoke.

You stop at the edge of the wreckage and feel the road waiting for an answer. This is not just another errand. The way you step forward here will shape what kind of cultivator you become: blade, healer, investigator, or maker.

"Choose carefully," you tell yourself. "This road is going to remember."

choices.label: Take up a staff and confront the bandits
Question merchants, guards, and frightened witnesses
Help repair wagons, harness, and road gear
Treat the wounded hidden in the roadside ditch
Return to the village square

title: Spears In The Dust
body: You follow boot prints past a burned waymarker. Men step from the scrub with stolen spears and road knives, but none of them look proud. Behind them, someone small is crying inside a covered cart.

opening: {enemy} spread across the road while a child sobs behind the cart canvas.
strike: You step inside a spear thrust and strike {enemy} for {damage} damage.
Dust jumps under your heel as your blow lands on {enemy} for {damage} damage.
focus: You force qi into your limbs and break the bandit line for {damage} damage.
enemyAttack: {enemy} answer with desperate, ugly strikes for {damage} damage.
victory: The last bandit drops his weapon and runs for the hill scrub.
defeat: A spear haft catches your ribs, and the bandits drag the cart deeper into dust.

title: The Missing Child
body: Inside the cart, a child in travel-stained silk clutches a cloud-marked jade pendant. They cannot be older than seven. When you cut the rope from their wrists, the pendant warms against your palm, and for one breath the air smells like rain over high mountains.

choices.label: Calm the child and send them back with the merchants

title: A Debt With No Name
body: The child will not give a family name, only a shaky promise that someone will remember. One merchant notices the jade and goes pale. Another mutters that no road bandit should dare touch a child carrying such a mark unless someone stronger made them do it.

choices.label: Search the abandoned road before returning home

title: Driven From The Road
body: The bandits fight like cornered dogs. You are forced back before you can reach the covered cart, but not before you see a black-thread token passed from one bandit to another. Their fear is not of you. That may matter later.

choices.label: Bind your bruises, then search what the bandits left behind

title: Whispers Beside Broken Wheels
body: You pour tea, listen more than you speak, and let frightened people contradict each other until the shape of the truth appears. A caravan guard saw a paper charm flare before the attack. A merchant heard the words mountain gate. A captured lookout says the bandits pay tribute to someone who wears blue cloud silk.

choices.label: Search the road for the object behind the rumors

title: The Manual In The Axle Box
body: You mend cracked wheel hubs, reset harness buckles, and patch spear cuts in leather. In a hidden axle box, wrapped in oilcloth, you find a field manual full of practical repairs for cloud-iron fittings. It is not a grand inheritance, but it is the kind of knowledge people kill to keep useful.

choices.label: Search the axle box again for what the manual was hiding

title: The Girl With Splintered Breath
body: The wounded girl is hidden under reeds beside the ditch, her silk sleeve dark with blood and her breathing uneven in a way you do not understand. She tries to tell you she is only a merchant's niece, then fails to hide the pale light moving under her skin. Your village medicine should not matter to someone like this, but pain has made her human enough.

choices.label: Scavenge the wreckage for clean cloth, water, and useful herbs

title: Medicine From A Broken Road
body:
You search under cart canvas, beside spilled grain, and between stones still warm from strange fire. Most of what you find is useless. A strip of clean inner lining becomes bandage. A cracked water jar gives enough to wash blood away. Near the ditch, cloud moss clings to shaded stones.

Aunt Lin would complain about the dirt, the wind, your knife, and your breathing. You hear her anyway.

"Medicine and poison can look nearly identical," you mutter, sorting leaves with shaking fingers.

choices.label: Prepare a cooling remedy with your herbalist training

title: A Pulse That Stops Fighting
body:
The remedy is crude, bitter, and made in a road ditch with a cracked bowl. Somehow, it works.

The pale light beneath her skin stops lashing against itself. Her breath still catches, but it catches like a hurt person now, not like a spirit lamp about to shatter.

She opens her eyes near midnight.

"You should have left," she whispers.

"I considered it. Briefly. Then you looked expensive to bury."

choices.label: Keep watch through the second night and change the bandages

title: Gao Xue's Promise
body:
By the third morning, the girl's pulse finally steadies. She sits against a broken wheel with your bandages wrapped under silk that was never meant to touch road dust.

"My name is Gao Xue," she says. The name lands carefully, like something dangerous being set on a table. "I cannot explain who wanted me taken. Not yet. But I know what you spent to keep me alive."

She presses a pale lotus hairpin into your hand. The metal is cool, but the carved petals hold a faint medicinal fragrance.

"When I can repay this, I will. If anyone asks, you found no one here. Especially not me."

Then she walks into the morning mist, slower than pride would like, but alive.

choices.label: Stay a little longer and search the abandoned road

recordNpcConversation.topic: Healed her over three days on the broken trade road. She gave her name and promised to repay the debt someday.

title: What The Dust Kept
body:
The road finally grows quiet enough to search properly. Under torn cart canvas and broken axle wood, you find a cracked pill bottle, two spirit stones wrapped in cloth, and a bronze token tied with black thread.

The token bears a cloud over a mountain. When you turn it north, it warms against your palm.

"This was not dropped by accident," you whisper.

choices.label: Return home and ask what the token symbol means

title: The Token No One Wants To Touch
body:
Back in the village, the bronze token draws eyes and silence. No one wants to be the first to name it. Old Ren studies the thread. Aunt Lin sniffs the metal like medicine gone bad. Guo refuses to put it near the forge fire. Mei watches the cloud mark as if it is watching back.

"Someone in this village knows what this means," you say. "Please. I need the truth before it finds me first."

choices.label: Ask Old Ren about the black thread

recordNpcConversation.topic: Studied the black-threaded token and said it looked like a travel claim for an immortal road.

choices.label: Ask Aunt Lin why the metal smells strange

recordNpcConversation.topic: Warned that the token carries clean qi and is not ordinary bandit loot.

choices.label: Ask Blacksmith Guo about the cloud-over-mountain mark

recordNpcConversation.topic: Recognized the cloud-over-mountain mark from old caravan hardware bound for the Azure Cloud Sect.

choices.label: Ask Shrine Keeper Mei why the token warms toward the north

recordNpcConversation.topic: Said the token is answering the mountain and that the Azure Cloud Sect may answer back.

choices.label: Wait with the token after gathering everyone's clues

title: Old Ren Names The Thread
body:
Old Ren turns the token over with the tip of his cane instead of touching it.

"Black thread means a carried claim. Caravan folk used to say such tokens buy passage on roads mortals do not own. If bandits had this, someone gave them courage they did not earn."

"Can you read the symbol?"

"No. But I have seen guards stand straighter when that cloud mark was painted on a sealed crate."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Old Ren's clue

title: Aunt Lin Smells Clean Qi
body:
Aunt Lin holds the token near a bowl of steeping herbs. The surface of the tea ripples before her hand moves.

"This is clean qi. Too clean for thieves. Too clean for anyone who still sweats for food."

"So it belongs to an immortal?"

"Or to someone who wants an immortal to come looking."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Aunt Lin's clue

title: Guo Remembers The Mark
body:
Guo sketches the cloud-over-mountain mark in soot on the forge stone.

"Saw this once on a crate axle bound north. The merchant would not say the sect name, but his guard did after too much wine. Azure Cloud."

"The Azure Cloud Sect?"

"If you have their token, either they lost something, or they are letting you carry it."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Guo's clue

title: Mei Watches The Token Breathe
body:
Mei lays the token beside the shrine incense. The smoke bends north without wind.

"It is answering the mountain. Quietly, but not weakly."

"Can the mountain answer back?"

"If this belongs to the Azure Cloud Sect, it may already be listening."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Mei's clue

title: The Immortal At The Door
body:
Three days pass. The token grows warmer each dawn.

On the fourth morning, the village dogs go silent. A blue-robed cultivator descends through the mist as if the road rose to meet his feet. He stops at your door and looks at the bronze token in your hand.

"So it chose a mortal after all. Fate is rarely polite. The Azure Cloud Sect opens its mountain gate in ten days. Bring the token. Climb if you dare."

You bow because your knees have already decided to bend.

"Why me?"

The immortal's smile is almost kind.

"Ask the mountain if you reach it."

choices.label: Begin preparing for the Azure Cloud mountain gate

title: A Chance From The Clouds
body: By nightfall the village is awake and afraid. The road trouble was not just bandits. Someone recognizes the cloud jade. Someone else has heard of blue-robed immortals gathering mortal candidates. Near midnight, a messenger arrives with a paper seal that refuses to burn in the cooking fire. The Azure Cloud Sect will test those with enough grit to climb. For the first time, the impossible has an address.

choices.label: Begin preparing for the Azure Cloud mountain gate

title: The Mountain Gate

image.alt: A steep mountain path rising toward the Azure Cloud Sect gate
image.caption: The Azure Cloud Sect waits above the jade steps.

body:
The Azure Cloud mountain gate does not look built so much as carved out of the mountain's first breath. Two stone pillars rise higher than any village watchtower, their dragon reliefs half-hidden by moss and cold mist. Between them, a jade stair climbs into cloud until the steps vanish, then reappear far above as a thin white scar across the cliffs.

Spiritual pressure leaks from the gate in slow waves. It is not qi you can gather. Not yet. It is weight: on your shoulders, in your teeth, behind your eyes. Around you, candidates pretend not to feel it. Clan heirs arrive in lacquered carriages. Merchant children clutch advice scrolls. Servants carry boxes of pills, spare robes, and weapons wrapped in silk.

Your village pack hangs from one shoulder. Your trial token rests against your palm, warmer than it should be.

A bronze bell sounds once from somewhere above the clouds. The exam has not begun, but the mountain is already sorting people.

choices.label: Continue up toward the registration crowd

title: The Outer Queue
body:
The first stair is wide enough for ten ox carts, but the crowd still finds a way to press shoulder to shoulder. Sect attendants in grey robes guide candidates into loose lines. Above them, bronze plaques mark origins you barely understand: clan recommendation, merchant bond, wandering token, mortal village exception.

That last one catches like a thorn.

A blue-silk heir near a lacquered carriage laughs whenever a poor candidate passes. A plain-robed girl stands near the stair shadow, breathing in sets of three. A merchant boy fumbles with a pack strap and tries to hide how badly his hands shake.

No examiner has called your name yet. For one quiet moment, you can decide how to enter this crowd.

choices.label: Scan the crowd before choosing where to stand

recordNpcConversation.topic: Noticed her calm breathing at the mountain gate before trouble could set your pace.

choices.label: Help the nervous boy whose pack is coming loose

recordNpcConversation.topic: Helped him fix his pack straps at the mountain gate before registration.

choices.label: Hold your ground when the blue-silk heir laughs at your village clothes

recordNpcConversation.topic: He mocked your village clothes at the mountain gate before registration.

choices.label: Ignore the crowd and join the registration line with your trial token

title: Faces Beneath The Gate
body:
You do not move at first. Mei taught you that stillness is sometimes the only way to hear what noise is hiding.

The crowd sorts itself when you stop trying to look at all of it. The blue-silk heir near the lacquered carriage laughs whenever a poor candidate passes, but his eyes keep checking who is watching. Trouble, then. Not because he is loud, but because he performs cruelty like a test.

A plain-robed girl stands near the stair shadow, breathing in sets of three. Her shoulders rise less each time the gate pressure rolls over the crowd. Friendly, perhaps, or at least disciplined enough not to waste fear.

A merchant boy fumbles with a pack strap and tries to hide how badly his hands shake. He is not dangerous. That may make him easy prey.

Two reactions follow. Liu Zhen's smile misses you because you move before his servants can hem you in. Han Yue gives the smallest nod, as if she saw you choose quiet ground instead of loud trouble.

choices.label: Continue toward the origin plaques

recordNpcConversation.topic: Spotted him at the mountain gate and recognized he might become an ally if kept away from harsher candidates.

title: Faces Lost In The Crowd
body:
You try to read the crowd, but the mountain gate presses at your skull and every silk robe, servant box, token cord, and anxious face blurs into noise. By the time you decide where to stand, the line has already shifted around you.

Two reactions follow. Liu Zhen's servants drift close enough that his laugh lands where everyone can hear it. The plain-robed girl near the stair shadow notices your late step, then looks away before helping can become attention.

You still find the registration line, but you enter it tired and watched.

choices.label: Continue toward the origin plaques

title: A Strap Before The Stair
body:
The merchant boy's pack slips sideways just as the line surges. A servant behind him clicks his tongue and a silk-robed candidate laughs, but no one reaches out.

You catch the strap before the pack drags him off balance.

"Higher," you say, tightening it across his shoulder. "If the weight pulls low, the stair will make it feel twice as heavy."

He blinks at you, startled by useful kindness.

"Qiao Min," he says quickly. "My father said alliances are expensive, but he forgot to mention straps. Thank you."

A few candidates notice. Some with approval. Some with the look of people counting weakness.

choices.label: Walk with Qiao Min toward the origin plaques

title: Blue Silk, Village Dust
body:
The blue-silk heir looks you over from patched cuff to worn shoe.

"The sect is generous this year," he says. "Even mountain weeds are allowed to climb."

A few candidates laugh because his robe costs more than their courage.

You touch the trial token in your palm and meet his eyes.

"If the mountain only wanted silk, it would test wardrobes."

The laughter thins. His smile stays, but it loses all warmth.

"Liu Zhen," someone whispers near you. "Liu clan. Do not make him remember you."

Too late. He already has.

choices.label: Move on before the argument becomes a scene

title: The Origin Plaques
body:
Past the outer crowd, the stair widens beneath five bronze plaques fixed into the cliff face. Each one hangs from chains as thick as your wrist. Mist beads along the carved words: Clan Recommendation, Merchant Bond, Wandering Token, Servant Sponsorship, Mortal Village Exception.

Most candidates know where to stand before they arrive. Clan heirs drift to polished stone lanes where attendants bow without being asked. Merchant children present stamped contracts. Servant-sponsored candidates keep their eyes low and their documents higher than their pride.

The last plaque waits at the edge of the stair, half-dark beneath a pine shadow.

Mortal Village Exception.

No one stands there yet. That may be mercy, warning, or both.

choices.label: Read the Mortal Village Exception plaque carefully
Ask a grey-robed attendant what exception means
Keep walking without staring
Stand beneath the wrong plaque and hope no one notices

title: The Mortal Exception
body:
You wait until the mist thins and read the small characters beneath the plaque.

Mortal Village Exception: token-bearers without clan backing may register if the token answers the mountain formation. No servant may speak for them. No sponsor may shield them. Their result belongs to them alone.

Two reactions follow. Qiao Min goes quiet beside you, realizing your path has fewer cushions than his. Liu Zhen notices the same thing and smiles like he has found a bruise.

choices.label: Step away before more candidates gather

title: Words In Mist
body:
The plaque's smaller characters blur beneath old patina and cold mist. You make out enough to know it concerns token-bearers without sponsors, but not enough to understand the rules.

Two reactions follow. A merchant candidate snorts when you lean closer for too long. Han Yue notices your frustration and shifts aside, giving you a clearer path forward without making a performance of helping.

choices.label: Leave the plaque and keep moving

title: The Attendant's Answer
body:
You bow just enough to be respectful without wasting the line's patience.

"Senior brother, what does Mortal Village Exception mean?"

The grey-robed attendant looks you over, then answers in a low voice.

"It means the sect did not invite your family. It invited your fate. Do not let anyone else touch your token before the jade table."

Two reactions follow. His warning gives you a useful chill. Liu Zhen's servant, who had been drifting too close, stops pretending not to listen.

choices.label: Grip the token and continue

title: A Question Too Loud
body:
You ask quickly, but the words come out louder than you mean them to.

"What does Mortal Village Exception mean?"

Several candidates turn. The attendant's mouth tightens.

"It means you should stand where the plaque tells you to stand. Next."

Two reactions follow. The crowd learns you do not know the rules. Qiao Min winces, then edges closer as if standing beside you can make the mistake smaller.

choices.label: Swallow the embarrassment and keep moving

title: The Wrong Chain
body:
You step beneath Merchant Bond because the line is shorter and the attendants are moving faster.

A clerk glances at your clothes, then at your token cord.

"Not this lane. Mortal exception. There."

The word there lands heavier than it should.

Two reactions follow. A few candidates laugh because they have been waiting for someone poorer than them to make the first mistake. Han Yue does not laugh. She only watches which faces enjoy it.

choices.label: Cross to the mortal exception lane

title: The Token Warms
body:
The closer you come to the gate formation, the warmer the Azure Cloud token becomes. At first it is only a coin left in sunlight. Then it presses heat into your palm like a coal wrapped in silk.

The stone beneath your shoes hums with a sound too low for hearing. Some candidates' tokens remain dull. Others pulse faintly beneath embroidered sleeves. One boy in red tries to hide a token that has gone completely cold.

An attendant's eyes sweep the lane.

The token is no longer proof that you were invited. It is starting to answer the mountain.

choices.label: Hide the token in your sleeve
Ask if anyone else's token is warming
Focus on the warmth and steady your breathing
Hold the token openly and keep walking

title: Heat Under Cloth
body:
You tuck the token into your sleeve only after watching where the attendants' eyes pause. The cloth hides the glow without blocking the formation's pull.

Two reactions follow. Liu Zhen's servant loses track of your token hand. Han Yue notices the timing and gives you another almost-nod.

choices.label: Walk into the gate pressure

title: A Clumsy Sleeve
body:
You push the token into your sleeve too quickly. The fabric catches, the token flashes blue-white, and an attendant's gaze snaps toward you.

Two reactions follow. The attendant marks something on a bamboo slip. Liu Zhen sees enough to know your token is active, and that seems to please him.

choices.label: Stop fumbling and face the gate pressure

title: Other Warm Tokens
body:
"Is anyone else's token warming?" you ask, keeping your voice low.

Qiao Min opens his hand. His token glows weakly, like a candle afraid of wind.

Han Yue does not show hers, but she answers.

"The mountain is testing whether the token knows you. Do not panic when it burns. Panic if it goes cold."

Two reactions follow. Qiao Min looks relieved to have words for his fear. Liu Zhen hears the exchange and decides you are gathering allies.

choices.label: Carry that warning into the gate pressure

title: A Breath Of Blue Heat
body:
You do not try to gather the token's heat. You only breathe around it the way Old Ren taught you to breathe around pain: acknowledge it, give it room, refuse to let it command you.

For one blink, a thread of blue warmth moves through your palm and settles behind your ribs.

Two reactions follow. Your body feels steadier, though the effort leaves your legs heavier. The gate formation seems to notice you noticing it.

choices.label: Step forward before your courage cools

title: The First Wave Of Pressure
body:
The lane passes beneath the outer arch. The moment your foot crosses the shadow, the mountain presses down.

It is not an attack. That almost makes it worse. The pressure is patient, ancient, and perfectly indifferent to whether your knees understand pride. Candidates gasp around you. Someone drops a scroll. Someone else vomits into a lacquered sleeve and pretends it did not happen.

The jade stair ahead is only a few body lengths away.

To reach registration, you must cross the arch without letting the mountain choose your posture for you.

choices.label: Set your feet and ground your stance
Regulate your breath before the pressure peaks
Help Qiao Min stay upright
Force yourself forward through the pressure

title: Stone Under The Feet
body:
You lower your weight the way the river stones taught you: knees soft, spine alive, breath held only long enough to become a root.

The pressure rolls over you. It bends your shoulders but does not take your feet.

Two reactions follow. A few candidates glance over, surprised a village body knows how to endure. Liu Zhen's smile tightens because this was supposed to be beneath his notice.

choices.label: Step beyond the arch

title: Knees In The Dust
body:
You try to brace, but the pressure finds every place your training is still only effort. One knee hits the stone before you can stop it.

Two reactions follow. Pain flashes up your leg. Qiao Min reaches out before thinking, then pulls back when an attendant's eyes pass over both of you.

choices.label: Stand and finish crossing

title: Breath Against The Mountain
body:
You breathe in three counts and release in six. The pressure does not lessen. You do.

For a moment, the mountain's weight becomes rhythm instead of panic.

Two reactions follow. Han Yue notices the breathing pattern and matches it for one step. The token warms again, softer this time, as if approving of restraint.

choices.label: Step beyond the arch

title: A Broken Breath
body:
You try to count your breath, but the pressure crowds the numbers out. Your inhale catches halfway. Your chest burns before the arch is behind you.

Two reactions follow. The token cools for one frightening heartbeat. Liu Zhen sees your stagger and looks satisfied enough to remember it later.

choices.label: Recover before the registration table

title: A Shoulder In The Arch
body:
Qiao Min's legs lock as the pressure peaks. You catch his elbow before he falls into the candidate behind him.

"Do not fight it all at once," you say. "Small steps."

He nods, pale and sweating, and moves when you move.

Two reactions follow. You spend strength you could have saved. But Qiao Min reaches the far side with you, and gratitude can be heavier than silver.

choices.label: Catch your breath near the jade table

title: Teeth Through Pressure
body:
You grit your teeth and walk.

The mountain presses. You keep walking.

Pain sparks behind your eyes. Your ribs feel too narrow for breath. Still, you cross the arch without kneeling.

Two reactions follow. A few candidates respect the refusal to bend. Han Yue's expression says endurance without wisdom is still a kind of wound.

choices.label: Reach the jade table hurting but upright

title: Before The Jade Table

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The jade table waits, and every name before yours changes the air.

body:
The registration table is close enough to touch, but the line slows until every candidate is forced to wait with their own nerves.

Examiner Su writes names with a brush that never splashes ink. Each token is placed on a jade dish. Some glow blue. Some stay dark. When a token goes dark, an attendant guides its owner away before the crowd can decide whether to pity or mock them.

Liu Zhen stands two places behind you, speaking softly to a servant. Qiao Min checks his pack strap again and again. Han Yue watches the brush more than the candidates.

The next name is almost yours.

choices.label: Watch Examiner Su's brush and token dish
Listen to the noble candidates whispering behind you
Speak quietly with the ally closest to you
Step forward when Examiner Su calls for the next token

title: Ink And Light

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The dish shines only when token and bearer answer together.

body:
You watch the brush, but the brush is not the secret. The dish is.

A token brightens only after the candidate touches it without flinching. When one girl lets her uncle place it down for her, the glow weakens. Examiner Su notices and writes a smaller mark beside her name.

Two reactions follow. You understand that the test has already begun. Han Yue sees you notice and finally speaks first.

"Good," she says. "Do not let anyone carry your fate for you."

choices.label: Place your own token when called

title: Too Much To Watch

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The line moves, and the pattern hides in plain sight.

body:
You try to follow the brush, the dish, the tokens, the attendants, and the candidates all at once. The details tangle until nothing stands out.

Two reactions follow. Examiner Su's gaze passes over you as if measuring distraction. Qiao Min whispers, "I think we just put the token down when told. Probably."

choices.label: Trust the simple rule and step forward

title: Silk Whispers

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Behind every name, someone is already ranking the competition.

body:
You let the noble candidates' whispers wash past as if you are not listening.

"Liu Zhen's uncle sent a letter to the Outer Sword Pavilion."

"Han Yue refused a carriage. Strange girl."

"The mortal exception has a live token? That should not happen often."

Two reactions follow. You gain names and hints, but Liu Zhen's servant notices your stillness. By the time the line moves, you know more than before and are trusted less.

choices.label: Step forward before they decide what you heard

title: A Quiet Word

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: A single quiet word can make the mountain feel less crowded.

body:
You speak without turning it into a scene.

If Qiao Min is near you, he exhales like a man remembering he is allowed to breathe.

"If I faint," he whispers, "tell my father I was robbed. It sounds better."

If Han Yue is closer, she keeps her eyes forward.

"The gate rewards people who know when to stand alone," she says. "It does not require them to be lonely."

Two reactions follow. The line feels less hostile. Liu Zhen sees that you are not as isolated as he hoped.

choices.label: Answer Examiner Su when your name is called

title: Names Before The Gate

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Names are written before the gate, and every robe in the crowd seems to weigh more than yours.

body:
A grey-robed examiner sits behind a jade table and checks each token without looking impressed by any of them. Rich candidates announce family names that make nearby servants bow. When your turn comes, you place the Azure Cloud trial token on the table.

The jade dish answers before Examiner Su can speak. Blue light spreads under the token in a clean circle, bright enough that the candidates behind you fall quiet.

Examiner Su's brush pauses.

"Village origin?"

"Yes."

A boy in blue silk laughs softly behind you.

"The mountain is generous this year."

Examiner Su finally looks up.

"This token is enough to admit you as a normal outer disciple. Robe, room, low stipend, no trial ranking. Or you may enter the full Azure Cloud examination. The token will still preserve your place if you fail, but only the trials can earn rewards, hall attention, and a better beginning."

choices.label: Use the token and enter as a normal outer disciple

recordNpcConversation.topic: Used the Azure Cloud token for safe outer disciple entry instead of entering the full Azure Cloud examination.

choices.label: Show the token and enter the full Azure Cloud examination

recordNpcConversation.topic: Chose the full Azure Cloud examination even though the token guaranteed safe outer disciple entry.

title: The Safe Robe

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The token opens the gate, but it does not make the mountain bow.

body:
You let the token's blue light stand for you.

"Outer disciple entry," Examiner Su says, writing the mark beside your name. "No trial ranking. No exam reward. Report with the other admitted candidates after the testing grounds clear."

A grey-robed attendant gives you a plain wooden tally. It is not shameful. It is a place inside the sect, and many mortals would trade years of life for it.

Two reactions follow. Qiao Min looks relieved that survival is allowed to be enough. Liu Zhen smiles as if he has found the word he wanted for you: lucky.

Han Yue watches the token, then you.

"Living through the gate is still living," she says. "Just remember that the sect remembers who chose to be measured."

choices.label: Follow the attendant toward the outer disciple dormitory

title: Hall Evaluations

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: After the core trials, the halls decide where your first real advantage may come from.

body:
Only after the core trials does Examiner Su call the surviving candidates back to the jade table. Dust, sweat, and sect qi cling to everyone differently now. Some stand taller. Some only stand because pride has not yet given them permission to fall.

Four faint symbols wake along the dish's rim: a crossed blade, a hammer over a cracked plate, a leaf inside a pill furnace, and a brush mark shaped like lightning.

"The trials decided whether you could enter," Examiner Su says. "A hall evaluation decides where the sect may invest in you first. Choose one test. Do not choose what sounds grand. Choose what your hands can answer."

choices.label: Request the Fighting Exam
Request the Crafting Exam
Request the Herbalist and Alchemy Exam
Request the Talisman Exam

title: The Martial Ring Announcement

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The martial path does not ask whether you entered safely. It asks where you should stand.

body:
Examiner Su lifts the crossed-blade symbol from the jade dish. It becomes a small bronze token, warm and heavy, marked with the number one.

"The fighting exam is a ranking tournament," he says. "The token guarantees sect entry. The ring decides starting rank. Unranked candidates enter as ordinary outer disciples. Bronze, Silver, Gold, and Jade Ring candidates receive better treatment, better stipends, and earlier eyes from the Martial Hall."

A second examiner opens a stone gate beside the registration table. Beyond it waits a square fighting ring carved directly into the mountain.

"First, a puppet seeding match. Then candidates. Control matters. Cruelty is recorded. Cowardice is also recorded."

Liu Zhen smiles as if the word rank was invented for him.

Han Yue glances at you once.

"Do not fight his shadow," she says quietly. "Fight the test."

choices.label: Step into the martial ring for the seeding match

title: Puppet Seeding Match

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: A wooden puppet waits without hatred, mercy, or impatience.

body:
An attendant carries out a wooden puppet with bronze joints and a blank face. Its arms hang loose until Examiner Su taps the jade dish. Then the puppet bows.

"One exchange," Examiner Su says. "Show stance, reaction, and control. This match decides your seed, not your worth. Do not mistake the two."

The puppet's bronze feet scrape once against the ring.

Liu Zhen laughs under his breath.

"A village body against sect wood. This should be educational."

Han Yue does not laugh. She watches your feet.

choices.label: Meet the puppet with village fist forms
Use your staff training to control the distance

title: A High Seed

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The puppet yields one step, and the crowd remembers your feet.

body:
The puppet moves like a dropped shadow. You do not try to overpower it. You let the first strike tell you its weight, then answer with the part of your training that survived bruises, cold mornings, and old corrections.

Wood cracks against stone. Not broken. Checked.

Two reactions follow. Examiner Su moves your bronze token higher on the bracket board. Han Yue's eyes sharpen with respect. Liu Zhen stops smiling because mockery is easier when the target cannot hit back.

choices.label: Enter round one with a better seed

title: A Low Seed

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Failure hurts, but the token still shines.

body:
The puppet is faster than a village lesson has any right to prepare you for. Its bronze wrist clips your guard aside, and the stone under your heel steals your balance.

You do not fall cleanly. That may be worse.

Two reactions follow. Liu Zhen laughs openly this time. Examiner Su does not. He only checks that you stand again before moving your bronze token to the lower bracket.

"Low seed," he says. "Not expelled. The token remains valid, and the ring is not finished with you."

choices.label: Enter round one from the lower bracket

title: Round One: A Desperate Opponent

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The first human opponent is not cruel. That makes the fight harder.

body:
Your first opponent is a thin commoner with wrapped knuckles and fear held tightly behind his eyes. He bows too fast.

"Chen Bo," he says. "No clan. No sponsor. If I rank, my village eats this winter."

The ring suddenly feels smaller.

When the gong sounds, he rushes with everything he owns: bad footwork, honest courage, and a punch thrown like a prayer.

choices.label: Win with control and end it cleanly
Overwhelm him before he can gather courage
Help him stand after you win

title: A Clean First Win

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Control is quieter than power, but the ring hears it.

body:
You let Chen Bo's courage reach you without letting it break you. One step aside. One palm to turn his shoulder. One sweep that sends him to the mat without smashing his face into stone.

Two reactions follow. Examiner Su marks the win without frowning. Han Yue nods once, as if restraint is a language she respects.

choices.label: Advance to the second round

title: A Messy First Win

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: A win can still cost breath, skin, and pride.

body:
You try to end it cleanly, but Chen Bo's desperation keeps changing shape. His shoulder clips your ribs. Your counter catches him harder than intended.

He falls. You win. Neither of you looks proud.

Two reactions follow. Examiner Su records the victory with a smaller mark. Liu Zhen smiles at the clumsiness, then stops when he realizes you are still advancing.

choices.label: Advance while catching your breath

title: A Hard Lesson

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The crowd respects strength faster than it respects kindness.

body:
You do not give Chen Bo time to become brave. You step into him, break his rhythm, and send him down hard enough that the breath leaves him in a thin sound.

Two reactions follow. Some candidates cheer because victory is easy to understand. Han Yue's expression cools. Examiner Su writes the win and another mark beside it.

choices.label: Advance under louder eyes

title: A Hand After The Gong

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Mercy is not weakness when everyone can see you already won.

body:
The fight is awkward, breathless, and honest. When Chen Bo falls, you offer your hand before the attendants can drag him away.

He stares at it, then takes it.

"Rank for both of us next time," he mutters.

Two reactions follow. Qiao Min looks openly relieved. Han Yue's respect settles into something warmer. Liu Zhen rolls his eyes as if kindness is a provincial disease.

choices.label: Advance with one more person hoping you win

title: Round Two: Clan Retainer

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The second opponent fights like someone trained to make another person's name look good.

body:
Your second opponent wears no silk, but the cut of his sleeves is too precise for poverty. A clan retainer. Someone trained from childhood to stand behind heirs and make their victories easier.

He bows correctly. His eyes do not.

The gong sounds. He does not rush. He circles, testing your lead foot, your breathing, the angle of your guard. His first attack is a feint inside a feint.

choices.label: Read the feint before committing
Break his rhythm with staff distance
Meet technique with stubborn endurance

title: Technique Answered

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The trained retainer expected ignorance. He found timing.

body:
The retainer's feint arrives dressed as certainty. You do not buy it.

Your answer lands before his true strike can unfold. Not beautiful. Not sect-polished. But correct.

Two reactions follow. The crowd makes a different sound this time. Not laughter. Calculation. Liu Zhen's servant leans close to whisper in his ear.

choices.label: Prepare for the semifinal pressure

title: A Bruised Advance

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Some victories are mostly proof that you refused to fall first.

body:
You cannot fully read him. You cannot fully stop him. So you endure.

He scores cleaner strikes. You take worse bruises. But the ring has edges, and fatigue teaches everyone. When he oversteps, you drive him out of bounds with more grit than grace.

Two reactions follow. Examiner Su marks the advance with a note beside it. Han Yue exhales as if she had been holding your breath for you.

choices.label: Limp toward the semifinal pressure

title: A Clean Loss

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The second round ends your bracket, but not your path.

body:
The retainer's feint takes your balance, then his real strike takes the space you meant to recover into. The ring edge arrives behind your heel. One more step and you are out.

The gong sounds.

Two reactions follow. You have lost the tournament bracket, but Examiner Su does not erase your name. Liu Zhen smirks. Han Yue watches to see whether you can accept defeat without shrinking.

choices.label: Stand straight for the ranking stone

title: Rival Pressure Before The Semifinal

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The next fight begins before the gong.

body:
Before the semifinal, Liu Zhen steps close enough that the attendants can pretend not to hear him.

"The token dragged you through the gate," he says. "The ring dragged you further. How many things must carry you before you admit your feet are decoration?"

His chosen fighter waits across the ring: a broad-shouldered candidate with a practice blade and a face trained not to show doubt.

The crowd listens. Of course it listens.

choices.label: Ignore Liu Zhen and steady your breath
Answer him publicly before the crowd
Study his chosen fighter instead of his insult

title: Semifinal: Liu Zhen's Blade

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: This opponent carries someone else's contempt into the ring.

body:
The chosen fighter bows with perfect form. His practice blade is dull, but his intent is not.

When the gong sounds, he attacks your hands first. Not your chest. Not your head. Your hands. Someone told him to take away the part of you that keeps surprising people.

Liu Zhen watches from the edge of the ring, smiling like this is already his victory.

choices.label: Endure the pressure and counter at the ring edge
Exploit the tell you noticed before the match
Trade blow for blow until someone breaks

title: A Smile Broken

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: A semifinal win changes the way enemies say your name.

body:
The chosen fighter overcommits by less than a finger's width. It is enough.

You turn the blade aside, step through the space behind it, and drive him out of the ring.

The gong sounds.

Two reactions follow. Liu Zhen's smile breaks cleanly. Han Yue does not cheer, but the look she gives you has the weight of one. Examiner Su moves your token into the final bracket.

choices.label: Face the final exchange

title: The Semifinal Wall

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The bracket ends, but the rank has not been spoken.

body:
You make him work for it. That matters. It does not change the gong.

The practice blade catches your guard, numbs your arm, and drives you over the ring line. The chosen fighter bows. Liu Zhen claps twice, slow and poisonous.

Two reactions follow. Han Yue watches you rise, not your loss. Examiner Su marks the semifinal finish with more ink than a failure should receive.

choices.label: Stand before the ranking stone

title: Final Exchange: Senior Disciple

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The final opponent does not need to defeat you. He only needs to reveal you.

body:
No candidate steps into the final ring.

A senior disciple does.

He wears no armor and carries no weapon. That is worse. His bow is polite enough to be terrifying.

"One exchange," Examiner Su says. "You are not expected to win. You are expected to show what remains when victory is impossible."

The senior disciple raises one hand. The air around his fingers tightens.

choices.label: Stay standing for one full exchange
Try to counter even though the gap is obvious

title: Martial Ranking Stone: Bronze Ring

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Bronze is not glory. It is proof the ring did not reject you.

body:
The ranking stone drinks the marks beside your name and answers with bronze light.

"Bronze Ring," Examiner Su announces. "Admitted with martial notice. Basic salve and standard outer stipend."

Two reactions follow. Liu Zhen treats the result like a small joke. Han Yue does not. Bronze is still metal, and metal can be tempered.

choices.label: Carry the Bronze Ring mark into outer disciple placement

title: Martial Ranking Stone: Silver Ring

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Silver light is bright enough for rivals to remember.

body:
The ranking stone glows silver, clean and cold.

"Silver Ring," Examiner Su announces. "Admitted with martial merit. Improved stipend. Manual selection after the first trial if you remain standing."

Two reactions follow. Qiao Min grins like he ranked with you. Liu Zhen looks away a heartbeat too late.

choices.label: Carry the Silver Ring mark into outer disciple placement

title: Martial Ranking Stone: Gold Ring

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Gold does not mean victory over the senior. It means the senior had to look.

body:
The senior disciple's exchange leaves your bones humming, but you are still on your feet when the ranking stone wakes.

Gold light spreads across its face.

"Gold Ring," Examiner Su announces. "Admitted with high martial merit. Martial Hall notice. Improved stipend. Technique selection after Trial One."

Two reactions follow. Han Yue's respect becomes impossible to mistake. Liu Zhen's rivalry stops feeling petty and starts feeling deliberate.

choices.label: Carry the Gold Ring mark into outer disciple placement

title: Martial Ranking Stone: Jade Ring

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Jade light is rare enough that even arrogance goes quiet.

body:
The senior disciple's palm stops one inch from your chest.

You are shaking. You are hurt. You are still standing with your breath intact.

The ranking stone answers in jade light.

For the first time since you reached the mountain gate, the entire crowd goes silent.

"Jade Ring," Examiner Su says, and even his voice changes. "Admitted with exceptional martial potential. Martial Hall priority notice. Technique selection after Trial One. Elder review pending."

Two reactions follow. Han Yue looks at you like the road just became interesting. Liu Zhen looks at you like a problem he can no longer postpone.

choices.label: Carry the Jade Ring mark into outer disciple placement

title: The Crafting Exam

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Not every test asks how hard you strike. Some ask what your hands understand.

body:
Examiner Su places a cracked bronze hinge on the jade table. It looks ordinary until the crack exhales a faint thread of blue smoke.

"This came from a low-grade spirit tool. Tell me why it failed, then stabilize it long enough that an artificer could repair it properly."

Some candidates lose interest at once. Others lean closer. The sect's swords may fly through clouds, but someone still has to know why metal fails.

choices.label: Listen for the flaw Guo would have heard
Stabilize the hinge with careful tool work

title: A Useful Hand

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The hinge stops smoking. That is enough for an elder to look twice.

body:
You do not polish the flaw. You respect it.

The hinge still looks ugly when you finish, but the blue smoke thins, then stops. Examiner Su turns it once, listening to a sound only trained hands seem to care about.

Two reactions follow. A Craft Hall attendant at the side of the table makes a note. A few silk-robed candidates look confused that fixing something could be worth respect.

choices.label: Carry the crafting mark into outer disciple placement

title: A Crack That Spreads

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The hinge breaks, but the lesson remains.

body:
You find the crack too late. The moment you press the hinge, blue smoke spits from the seam and the bronze snaps with a small, final sound.

Two reactions follow. The crowd loses interest quickly. Examiner Su sets the broken pieces aside without anger.

"Failed," he says. "You saw that it mattered, at least. The token remains valid."

choices.label: Accept the failed mark and continue to outer disciple placement

title: The Herbalist And Alchemy Exam

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Bitter leaves, clean hands, and a life measured in breaths.

body:
A Medicine Hall attendant brings a jade tray of five herbs and one small brass cup. One herb cools blood. One wakes qi. One leaves poison in the lungs if cut badly.

Examiner Su gestures to a pale candidate seated near the table, still shaking from the gate pressure.

"Prepare a stabilizing remedy. Do not make him stronger. Make him safe. Alchemy begins with restraint."

choices.label: Identify the correct herbs before touching the knife
Remove the impurity before the remedy turns harsh

title: A Remedy That Does Not Boast

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The best medicine leaves room for the body to recover itself.

body:
The cup smells bitter, clean, and unambitious. That is why it works.

The pale candidate drinks. Color returns slowly to his mouth. He does not leap up stronger than before. He simply stops shaking.

Two reactions follow. The Medicine Hall attendant's expression softens by half a breath. Han Yue watches you as if kindness and precision have become the same language.

choices.label: Carry the herbalist mark into outer disciple placement

title: Too Bitter, Too Fast

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: A failed remedy can still teach the shape of caution.

body:
The remedy darkens too quickly. The Medicine Hall attendant removes the cup before the patient can drink.

"Not poison," she says. "But not medicine. Not yet."

Two reactions follow. Shame burns hotter than the brass cup. Examiner Su writes a smaller mark beside your name, then taps the glowing token.

"Failed. Still admitted."

choices.label: Accept the failed mark and continue to outer disciple placement

title: The Talisman Exam

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: Ink, paper, breath, and a door you are not ready to open.

body:
The fourth symbol on the jade dish brightens when you choose it. A talisman brush is placed before you, its handle heavier than wood should be. Beside it lies a strip of yellow paper with one simple mark already drawn at the top.

Examiner Su studies you for a long moment.

"Talismans require spiritual control, not only a steady hand. You may copy the mark. Understand this: almost no mortal candidate succeeds. Curiosity is permitted. Arrogance is expensive."

The crowd grows quiet for a different reason now. Even Liu Zhen watches.

choices.label: Attempt to copy the spirit mark

title: A Line That Almost Lives

image.alt: A village youth registering before the Azure Cloud Sect gate while noble candidates watch
image.caption: The paper breaks, but the idea remains.

body:
The brush touches paper.

For one breath, the line almost lives.

Then the mark twists out of your control. The yellow paper splits with a sound like ice cracking, and a curl of harmless smoke rises between your fingers.

Two reactions follow. Examiner Su covers the broken talisman before anyone can laugh.

"Failed," he says. "Expected. But you stopped when the line broke instead of forcing qi you do not own. Remember that after your first breakthrough. Talismans may answer you later."

Han Yue looks at the smoke with open curiosity. Liu Zhen looks annoyed that even your failure received instruction.

choices.label: Keep the lesson and continue to outer disciple placement

title: The Elders Look Down

image.alt: Azure Cloud elders and candidates gathered before the sect gate
image.caption: Before the first trial, some candidates are measured by eyes older than the mountain road.

body:
Registration does not end with ink. Above the gate, several elders sit behind hanging veils of cloud, their sleeves still as stone. A few candidates are called forward immediately: clan heirs with polished foundations, medicine apprentices with scented sleeves, weapon children whose palms already know calluses.

You stand among the unclaimed names and feel every village bruise, every river stone, every bitter root and forge spark press quietly beneath your skin.

A senior disciple raises his voice.

"Those who have already tempered a path may step forward. Those who have not will follow me up the mountain. Listen well. The sect rewards talent, but it punishes ignorance faster."

choices.label: Step forward when the Martial Hall elder asks for tempered bodies
Present your herb work when the Medicine Hall elder asks for steady hands
Show the Craft Hall elder what Guo taught you about honest iron
Remain with the other unclaimed candidates and follow the senior disciple

recordNpcConversation.topic: He guided unclaimed candidates up the mountain and explained the outer sect rules before the first trial.

title: A Body Worth Testing
body:
The Martial Hall elder does not ask for your name first. He asks you to stand.

You set your feet the way Old Ren taught you: not pretty, not proud, hard to move. The elder's gaze drops to your knuckles, your shoulders, the old ache in your stance.

"Village training," he says.

"Yes."

"Crude. Useful. You have entered Mortal Middle before touching sect qi. Do not waste that. If you survive the first trial, report to the Martial Hall before choosing common lessons. I will see whether your bones are merely stubborn or worth tempering."

choices.label: Bow and return to the candidates below the stair

title: Hands That Know Bitter Work
body:
The Medicine Hall elder has you sort three leaves on a jade tray. Two are harmless. One would leave heat in the lungs for a week.

You do not reach quickly. Aunt Lin's voice lives in your fingers: look again.

The elder's veil shifts as if she smiled.

"A mortal village taught you caution. Good. Cultivators ruin themselves with pills because they think power excuses carelessness. If you pass the first trial, come to the Medicine Hall. I can use a disciple who hesitates for the right reason."

choices.label: Bow and return to the candidates below the stair

title: The Sound Of Honest Iron
body:
The Craft Hall elder tosses you a dull buckle with a hairline crack hidden beneath old polish.

"Tell me why this failed."

You turn it once, hearing Guo's hammer in memory. Bad iron complains before it fails.

"It was repaired to look whole, not to hold weight."

The elder laughs once, short and pleased.

"A poor answer would blame the metal. A useful answer blames the hand. Pass the trial, village child. The Craft Hall wastes nothing, and perhaps that includes you."

choices.label: Bow and return to the candidates below the stair

title: Rules Beneath The Clouds
body:
Senior Brother Wei leads the unclaimed candidates through the lower gate, neither cruel nor comforting.

"No fighting outside marked grounds. No stealing pills, manuals, spirit stones, or beasts. No entering sealed paths because a cousin dared you. Contribution points buy food, manuals, room upgrades, and forgiveness for small mistakes. They do not buy back a severed hand."

Someone laughs nervously. Wei does not.

"Outer disciples bow to elders, obey hall stewards, and keep their tokens visible. If a senior challenges you in the arena, you may refuse once without shame. Refuse twice and people will decide what your courage is worth."

You look up at the buildings stepping into cloud. You have not been chosen early. That does not mean the mountain has finished measuring you.

choices.label: Join the candidates gathering below the first trial stair

title: Waiting Ground
body:
Candidates gather below the first stair. Some stretch in practiced silence. Some compare pills. Some pretend they are not afraid. The blue-silk boy keeps glancing at your patched cuffs as if poverty might spread by eye contact.

Rumors move through the crowd: the first trial crushes weak bodies, the second exposes shallow minds, the third has sent noble heirs home bleeding, and the elders keep a hidden score no one announces.

choices.label: Listen to the blue-silk heir mocking village candidates

recordNpcConversation.topic: He mocked your village origin before the first trial and treated the exam like inheritance.

choices.label: Speak with the quiet commoner counting breaths

recordNpcConversation.topic: She warned that the first stair tests whether fear can disrupt breath.

choices.label: Help the nervous merchant child secure his pack straps

recordNpcConversation.topic: Helped him secure his pack before the exam; he whispered that candidates sometimes vanish during the third trial.

choices.label: Step onto the first pressure stair

title: Silk And Teeth
body:
The blue-silk heir speaks loudly enough for half the waiting ground to hear.

"A village token. How rustic. Did the sect begin accepting lost farm tools as candidates?"

You look at his polished boots, then at the stair disappearing into mist.

"If the mountain wanted boots tested, yours would be climbing alone."

His smile thins. Good. He has learned your face.

choices.label: Return to the waiting ground

title: The Quiet Count
body:
The quiet girl breathes in sets of three. Her robe is plain, but the cloth is clean and patched with the same care Aunt Lin gives bandages.

"Do not let them set your pace," she says without opening her eyes. "The stair listens to panic."

"You sound like someone who has failed it before."

"My brother did. I listened when he came home."

choices.label: Return to the waiting ground

title: Loose Straps
body:
The merchant child fumbles with a pack nearly as large as his chest. You tighten one strap and shift the weight higher.

"Thank you," he says, face pale. "My father bought three advice scrolls. They all disagree."

"What do they agree on?"
