"That the third trial is where polite people stop being polite."

choices.label: Return to the waiting ground

title: The First Trial Begins
body:
The examiner raises one hand. The jade steps answer with a pressure that rolls down the mountain like invisible rain. Some candidates gasp. Liu Zhen laughs too quickly. Han Yue lowers her shoulders and breathes.

The first trial is not a race. That does not stop half the crowd from trying to make it one.

choices.label: Climb steadily and conserve your breath
Rush ahead before the crowd can react

title: A Measured Ascent
body: You climb slowly, counting each breath. The pressure of the mountain presses against your bones, but your heart remains calm.

choices.label: Continue toward the first trial platform

title: Iron On The Steps
body: The reinforced staff bites against wet stone whenever your balance falters. It is only mortal iron and village workmanship, but under the mountain pressure that small certainty keeps your breathing even.

choices.label: Continue toward the first trial platform

title: A Reckless Ascent
body: You surge upward, earning surprised looks from the other hopefuls. The mountain pressure bites into your lungs, but for one bright moment you feel a thread of qi answer your ambition.

choices.label: Force yourself toward the first trial platform

title: The First Platform
body: A stone tablet waits beneath a bent pine. Its carved words shimmer faintly: To seek the Dao, first know the weight of desire.

choices.label: Trace the hidden qi current in the carved words
Place your palm on the tablet

title: A Hidden Current
body: The tablet's surface is cold, but beneath the characters you sense a patient current of qi circling like mist around a peak. You follow it for one breath and understand a fraction more than the others.

choices.label: Place your palm on the tablet

title: The First Trial
body:
The tablet does not teach gently. The qi of the Azure Cloud mountain pours through the stone and into your arms like cold rain forced through cracked earth. This is not the thin warmth of village elixirs. This is abundance, heavy enough to bruise.

Your mortal body panics first. Breath catches. Knees shake. Every careless day of training becomes a gap the mountain tries to split open.

Then Old Ren's stance holds your bones in line. Aunt Lin's warnings keep you from swallowing the power whole. Mei's breath count gives the storm a rhythm. Guo's lessons remind you that pressure can either break a flaw or close it.

Something in your flesh gives way. Something stronger answers.

You break through to Mortal Middle.

choices.label: Endure the breakthrough and let the tablet finish judging you

title: After The First Trial
body:
Cold light fades from the tablet. You are still standing. Several candidates are not.

Liu Zhen stares at you as if your breakthrough insulted him personally.

"Luck," he says.

Han Yue gives you the smallest nod.

Above the platform, Examiner Su marks your name with a second stroke instead of one.

"Mortal Middle during the first trial," he says. "Crude foundation. Unusual endurance. Continue."

choices.label: Walk toward the second trial mirrors

title: Second Trial: Cloud Mirror
body:
The next platform holds seven bronze mirrors filled with slow white cloud. Candidates step before them and see different things: family banners, dead parents, piles of pills, empty hands.

Examiner Su's voice carries over the platform.

"The mirror does not ask what you want. It asks whether you can see clearly while wanting it."

choices.label: Study the smallest flaw in the mirror's cloud pattern
Anchor your breath and refuse the images

title: Clear Cloud
body:
The mirror shows your village smaller than a thumbprint beneath the mountain. For a breath, shame tries to make it tiny. Then Guo's hammer, Aunt Lin's bitter table, Mei's ash line, and Old Ren's cane sharpen in your memory.

Small is not worthless. Small is where roots begin.

The cloud parts.

choices.label: Proceed to the survival ground

title: Cloud In The Lungs
body:
The mirror catches you with images of silk robes, laughing heirs, and your own hands empty at the village gate. Your breath stumbles. Then it returns, rough but yours.

The mirror does not open kindly, but it opens.

choices.label: Proceed to the survival ground

title: Third Trial: Mistwood Ground
body:
The third platform is not a platform at all. It is a mist-filled grove sealed by formation light. Examiner Su gestures toward the trees.

"Each candidate faces what the formation draws to them. Survive, retreat, or be carried out. Rank is not given to clean robes."

Somewhere in the fog, something growls.

choices.label: Enter the mistwood with your guard raised

title: First Breath Of The Dao
body: A thread of qi settles inside your meridians. When the light fades, a wooden token rests in your palm, and the first layer of the Azure Cloud Breathing Method echoes in your mind.

choices.label: Train the Azure Cloud Breathing Method under the pine

title: Breath Beneath The Pine
body: You kneel beneath the bent pine and follow the manual's first circulation route. The mountain pressure no longer feels like a wall. It feels like a teacher pressing one finger against your chest.

choices.label: Stand and present your circulation to the elders

title: Outer Disciple
body:
The watching elder gives the smallest nod. A path opens through the mist toward the outer sect courtyard. Behind you, the exam grounds continue judging the hopeful and the broken. Ahead, dormitories, rules, missions, rivals, and real cultivation wait.

Your village road has ended. Your sect road has begun.

choices.label: Follow the misty path toward the outer sect

title: Mist Wolf Ambush
body: The path narrows between old pines. A low growl rolls through the fog, and a pale spirit wolf steps from the roots with its hackles raised.

opening: Fog curls between the old pines as {enemy} lowers its head and claims the path.
Wet needles shift underfoot while {enemy} circles through the white mist.
strike: You drive forward over slick roots and strike {enemy} for {damage} damage.
Your blow cuts through drifting fog and catches {enemy} for {damage} damage.
You use a pine trunk to steady your footing, then hit {enemy} for {damage} damage.
focus: You pull qi through your breath until the mist trembles, then strike {enemy} for {damage} damage.
Cold fog beads on your sleeves as qi gathers in your arm and lands for {damage} damage.
The mountain pressure tightens around your ribs, but you force qi into the blow and deal {damage} damage.
enemyAttack: {enemy} springs from the fog and rakes across your guard for {damage} damage.
A pale shape flashes between the roots; {enemy} bites in for {damage} damage.
{enemy} vanishes behind a pine, then crashes into your side for {damage} damage.
victory: {enemy} stumbles in the wet grass, its growl fading into the fog.
The old pines fall silent as {enemy} collapses beneath the mist.
defeat: The fog swallows the path as {enemy} drives you back toward the trial stones.

title: Third Trial Blood
body:
The mist wolf collapses into the wet grass. Its spirit core flickers once in your palm before going still. Somewhere beyond the trees, the exam bell rings again.

You are bruised, breathing hard, and no longer able to pretend the sect tests are symbolic.

choices.label: Study the spirit core and steady your breath

title: Preparing The First Bottleneck
body:
The mist wolf core is too weak to force a realm breakthrough, but its fading qi teaches you how unstable power disperses. You mark that lesson in your heart for the day you challenge a true boundary.

At the edge of the grove, someone groans beneath a fallen branch. It is Qiao Min, the nervous merchant child, one arm pinned and his token just out of reach.

choices.label: Approach the hidden trial at the grove edge

title: Hidden Trial: The Pinned Candidate
body:
Formation mist curls around Qiao Min's trapped arm. Liu Zhen appears between the trees, robe torn but pride intact. He sees the fallen token and smiles.

"Leave him. The bell is nearly done. Help me reach the exit first and my family will remember your sense."

Qiao Min tries not to beg. That makes it worse.

choices.label: Free Qiao Min even if it costs your ranking

recordNpcConversation.topic: Freed him during the hidden character trial instead of chasing rank.

choices.label: Reject Liu Zhen but take the fastest safe path out
Accept Liu Zhen's offer and hurry for rank

recordNpcConversation.topic: Accepted his offer during the hidden trial, gaining advantage at a moral cost.

title: A Hand Under The Branch
body:
The branch shifts one finger-width at a time. Qiao Min bites through a cry. When he finally pulls free, the formation bell is already ringing the last count.

Examiner Su is waiting at the exit.

"Late," he says.

Then his gaze flicks to Qiao Min walking beside you.

"Noted."

choices.label: Stand before the ranking stone

title: No Bought Path
body:
You do not take Liu Zhen's bargain. You also do not throw your life away trying to solve every wound in the mist. The exit opens under your hand with seconds to spare.

Liu Zhen arrives after you, face pale with fury.

"Village luck again," he says.

"You should buy better luck."

choices.label: Stand before the ranking stone

title: A Clean Robe, A Dirty Hand
body:
Liu Zhen's shortcut works. You reach the exit early, but the mist clings to your sleeves with a cold that does not fade.

Examiner Su looks at you for one heartbeat too long.

"Efficient," he says.

It does not sound like praise.

choices.label: Stand before the ranking stone

title: Ranking Stone
body:
Names flare across the ranking stone in cloud-white light. Noble heirs search for their places first. Servants whisper. Failed candidates are led away with blank faces.

Your name appears lower than Liu Zhen's and higher than half the crowd expected. Beside it, a second mark blooms: First Trial Breakthrough.

Examiner Su reads the result aloud.

"Accepted as an outer disciple. Foundation rough. Endurance notable. Character under observation."

From somewhere near the elder seats, you feel attention settle on you like a hand between the shoulders.

Then four smaller symbols wake beneath your name: blade, hammer, leaf, and talisman brush. The core trials are over. Now the halls may ask what else you can become.

choices.label: Receive the outer disciple token and first breathing method
Request one hall evaluation before outer disciple placement

title: Outer Sect Courtyard
body: Morning mist drifts between dormitories, training platforms, and the assignment hall. Outer disciples hurry past with manuals under one arm and sect task slips in the other. For the first time, the day is yours to spend.

choices.label: Train the Azure Cloud Breathing Method at the morning platform
Study Pine Shadow Step with the footwork disciples
Visit the assignment hall board
Return to your room
Rest in the outer disciple dormitory
Spend a free hour around the outer sect paths
Ask an elder about your first real bottleneck

title: Morning Breathing Platform
body: You sit among rows of new disciples and follow the Azure Cloud circulation route. Each breath draws a little more mountain qi into your meridians, though the effort leaves your body warm and heavy.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Pine Shadow Lesson
body: A senior outer disciple marks footwork patterns in the dust. Your first attempts are clumsy, but by dusk your steps begin to turn with the wind instead of against it.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Assignment Hall
body: The assignment hall smells of ink, wet wood, and ambition. A steward slides you a thin bamboo slip: gather frost-vein herbs from the lower grove before another disciple claims the task.

choices.label: Search the lower grove carefully
Rush the task before sunset

title: A Thread Beneath The Frost
body: You slow your breathing until the grove's cold qi separates into faint threads. One thread bends beneath a fallen root, revealing a second cluster of frost-vein herbs and an old pill bottle wrapped in oilcloth. The steward's eyes sharpen when you return with more than he expected.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Frost-Vein Herbs
body: You kneel beside a shaded stream and find the pale herbs where the steward said they would grow. The hall pays a modest reward, and someone has tucked a minor qi pill into the bundle.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Thorns Under Frost
body: Careful intent is not the same as trained eyes. You mistake pale thorns for herb stems and only notice when they tighten around your wrist. You return with enough herbs to pass the task, but the grove takes blood for the lesson.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Before The Bell
body: You move hard and clean through the lower grove, trusting your legs where another disciple might trust caution. By sunset your robe is torn and your lungs burn, but the steward cannot argue with a full bundle delivered before the bell.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: A Hasty Task
body: You return scratched, tired, and holding a smaller bundle than expected. The steward pays you anyway, but his raised eyebrow says the sect remembers careless work.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Outer Disciple Dormitory

image.alt: A quiet wooden outer disciple room with a bed, desk, incense burner, and cloud view
image.caption: Your room is plain, quiet, and finally yours.

body: Your assigned room is narrow, plain, and blessedly quiet. By morning your breathing has steadied and some of the strain has left your bones. The room is small enough to feel like a cell, but it is yours.

choices.label: Spend time in your room — cultivate, study, or write
Return to the courtyard

title: Outer Sect Paths
body: The outer sect is larger than the courtyard first promised. Stone paths wind between lecture halls, work yards, medicine plots, and quiet cliffs where new disciples pretend not to be homesick.

choices.label: Visit the scripture pavilion threshold
Help weed the medicine garden
Ask the medicine senior sister for work that earns instruction
Bring unusual residues to the medicine senior sister
Watch sparring at the blade-scarred arena
Visit the Craft Hall forge
Explore the lower grove for useful finds
Walk to the cold north face of the lower grove
Walk to the cloud edge overlook
Follow where the black-thread token has been pointing all morning
Head down the mountain road — Iron Bridge Town is a day's walk south
Return to the main courtyard

title: Craft Hall Forge
body: The Craft Hall does not sound like a village forge. It breathes in furnace rows: bellows, hammer falls, hissed quenching troughs, clerks counting tool tokens, senior disciples watching for waste. The work here is cleaner than Luo's smithy and stronger than anything Guo could build with village iron, but the sect charges for access until a disciple has standing worth more than spirit stones.

choices.label: Pay the outer forge fee and rent a supervised bench (1 spirit stone)

messages: The clerk takes one spirit stone and stamps your name beside an outer forge bench. For now, Craft Hall strength comes rented by the hour.

choices.label: Use your Craft Hall standing to take a bench without paying

messages: The clerk checks your Craft Hall marks and waves the fee aside. Reputation has become a key, at least for the outer benches.

choices.label: Speak with Instructor Han Qingshi about paid lessons

recordNpcConversation.topic: Met him at the Craft Hall forge, where he sells basic lessons and withholds better patterns until a disciple proves useful.

choices.label: Take the free coal-sorting assignment instead of paying
Return to the outer sect paths

title: A Rented Forge Bench
body: Your assigned bench is narrow, but the tools are clean and the heat holds steady. A senior disciple watches the line for mistakes expensive enough to become lessons. You do not get freedom here. You get capacity: a proper anvil, a real quench, and permission to attempt work that road tools could never safely hold.

choices.label: Practice safe heat and honest inspection
Step back from the bench

title: A Bench Earned By Standing
body: No one smiles when the fee is waived. That is not how the Craft Hall shows approval. Instead, the clerk assigns you a bench without delay, and the senior disciple watching the furnace line corrects your grip instead of asking whether you paid. It is a small difference. It matters.

choices.label: Use the free bench for disciplined repair practice
Return to the forge floor

title: Instructor Han Qingshi
body:
Han Qingshi stands beside a rack of cracked practice fittings with his sleeves rolled above burn-scarred wrists. He looks at your token, then at your hands.

"Spirit stones buy instruction," he says. "Reputation buys attention. Quests buy trust. Do not confuse the three."

choices.label: Pay for basic forge safety instruction (1 spirit stone)

messages: Han makes you repeat the safety forms until your wrists stop drifting. Boring lessons are often the ones that keep fingers attached.

choices.label: Pay for field gear reinforcement patterns (2 spirit stones)

messages: Han sells you two approved patterns: one for reinforcing a staff with bone, and one for binding rough materials without lying to yourself about their strength.

choices.label: Pay for spirit material handling instruction (3 spirit stones, Craft Hall reputation 2)

messages: Only after checking your Craft Hall marks does Han unlock the stronger lesson. Sect patterns are not secret because they are complicated; they are secret because mistakes waste spirit material.

choices.label: Ask for a Craft Hall work assignment
Return to the forge floor

title: Coal, Flux, And Slag
body:
Han points you toward three bins and one rule: anything useful placed in the waste bin will be counted against you twice. The work is hot, filthy, and humiliatingly specific. Clean coal has a sound. Flux stone leaves dust on the thumb. Slag can glitter enough to fool a greedy beginner.

By the end, your hands are black and your eye is better.

choices.label: Report the sorted bins to Han

messages: Han checks the bins, finds only one mistake worth mentioning, and marks your name under useful hands.

title: A Cold Thread In The Leaves
body:
You leave the main path and let the grove's small sounds sort themselves out: water over stone, insects under bark, qi gathering where frost should not last. Beneath a root shelf you find a cluster of frost-vein herbs, a patch of cloud moss, and a forgotten pill bottle sealed in wax.

This is why disciples wander even when no steward is watching. The forest keeps rewards for people who notice what others step over.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Useful Green
body:
The lower grove does not hand over treasure loudly. You crouch near damp stones, compare leaf veins, and gather a clean bundle of frost-vein herbs and cloud moss without tearing the roots.

It is not a legendary inheritance. It is better than empty hands, and it teaches your fingers what sect herbs feel like before a senior sister is watching.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Underbrush And Old Bones
body: You push through the lower grove and quickly learn that immortal forests still have thorns. By the time you find your way back to the marked path, your sleeves are snagged, but your pouch holds cloud moss and a small beast bone half-buried under leaves.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Scripture Pavilion Threshold
body: A grey-robed clerk guards the pavilion doors with a brush, a ledger, and the bored patience of someone who has refused a thousand ambitious disciples before breakfast. The open shelves near the entrance hold only copied fragments, but even fragments can cut open a mind.

choices.label: Copy breathing annotations from a worn manual
Ask the clerk which rules new disciples break first
Step back from the pavilion

title: Borrowed Ink
body: You copy until your wrist aches. Most of the notes are mundane reminders about posture and breath, but one line describes qi as weather inside the bones. The phrase follows you long after the ink dries.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: The Clerk's Warning
body:
The clerk does not look up from the ledger.

"Do not steal pills, do not duel near the kitchens, do not insult inner disciples, and never mistake borrowed sect property for destiny."

Then he pauses and marks your name neatly.

"Listening is rarer than talent."

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Medicine Garden Work
body: The medicine garden smells of wet soil and sharp green leaves. A senior sister points you toward a row of stubborn spirit weeds whose roots cling like little fists around the frost-vein herbs.

choices.label: Pull each weed slowly and preserve the roots
Finish quickly before your back gives out
Return to the outer sect paths

title: Roots Intact
body:
By sunset the senior sister inspects the row and gives a rare approving hum. She presses a minor qi pill into your palm.

"Cultivation also means learning which roots should not be torn."

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Dirt Under The Nails
body: You finish the row, but several frost-vein leaves droop in the aftermath. The senior sister pays the token stone for your labor and says nothing, which somehow weighs more than a scolding.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Clean Hands, Clean Roots
body:
The senior sister studies you for a long moment after hearing your request.

"Instruction is not a wage," she says. "It is a debt. If I teach you a recipe and you use it carelessly, the person harmed by it will not know my name, but the fault still reaches me."

She sets three trays on the bench: dried frost-vein leaf, road wound moss, and a small dish of dark flakes scraped from a viper bite scar.

"Sort them. Throw away anything that looks impressive but smells wrong. Then copy the ratios until your wrist stops trying to hurry."

choices.label: Accept the corrected recipe notes

title: Mist That Leaves A Debt
body:
The senior sister does not touch the mist residue until she has wrapped her fingers in treated cloth.

"Residue is honest," she says. "It tells you what the body failed to digest. A crude cultivator chases stronger medicine. A living cultivator learns how to clean up after it."

She shows you how to bind the residue into a calming draught, then how to cut a meridian tonic with enough water-aspected herb that it mends without scouring.

choices.label: Study the residue-control recipes

title: Blade-Scarred Arena
body: At the arena, older outer disciples trade blows on stone scored by years of practice weapons. Each exchange is quick, ugly, and practical. No one here moves like the songs say heroes move.

choices.label: Study how Pine Shadow Step is used under pressure
Focus on how the losers recover their guard
Ask Senior Disciple Wei about the Iron Body method
Something in your Pine Shadow Step is ready to change — follow the feeling
Return to the outer sect paths

title: Footwork In The Dust
body: The lesson is not in the winning strike. It is in the half-step before it, when one disciple lets the opponent's force pass through empty air. Your own feet remember the pattern before your mind can name it.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: After The Hit
body: A disciple takes a practice blade to the ribs, staggers, and still raises his guard before breathing again. The watching crowd laughs, but you understand the hidden lesson: survival often begins after pride has already lost.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Cloud Edge Overlook
body: Beyond the last pine, the mountain falls away into a white sea of cloud. The sect sounds grow distant here: bells softened by mist, training shouts reduced to memory, your own breath louder than either.

choices.label: Meditate until the clouds stop feeling separate from you
Watch the paths below for useful patterns
Let the wind qi trace its patterns into your meridians
Return to the outer sect paths

title: Mist In The Meridians
body: You breathe with the slow movement of the cloud sea. For a few precious moments, your meridians loosen and the sect no longer feels like a place you must conquer. It feels like weather you might learn to survive.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Paths Beneath The Cloud
body: From above, the outer sect reveals its habits. Couriers avoid the kitchens at noon, senior disciples cut through the west arcade, and the quietest path to the assignment hall runs behind the medicine sheds.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Words Before The Bottleneck
body: The elder listens to your report and taps the mist wolf core with one finger. Minor stage breakthroughs may be forced with enough qi, but true realm bottlenecks demand timing, resources, and a foundation that will not crack under pressure.

choices.label: Ask what resource is needed to crack Mortal Middle
Present the completed Mist-Core Qi Draught and ask how to use it
Ask how to prepare for harder bottlenecks without cracking your foundation
Return to the courtyard and keep preparing

title: What The Bottleneck Needs
body:
The elder sets the mist wolf core down.

"The Mist-Core Qi Draught is a rough thing on its own. Wolf-core qi floods in, your meridians receive it, and then it disperses before it can do what you need. What focuses that flood is ice-vein herb — it slows the dispersal, sharpens the pressure, and gives you a window to push forward."

He tilts his head toward the lower grove.

"It does not grow near the path. The north face of the grove, where the stone cliff keeps permanent shadow — that is where you will find it. The roots go into the rock. Harvest the stalks cleanly or you will break them and lose the sap."

He does not offer to come with you. That part is yours.

choices.label: Head toward the cold north face of the grove now
Thank the elder and return to the courtyard

title: Medicine For The Wall
body:
The elder hears the question without smiling.

"Good. Wanting to survive the next wall before you touch it is the first sign you may deserve to reach it."

He draws a simple decoction pattern in dark ink: not a pill for forcing qi upward, but a stabilizing medicine that braces the foundation before a bottleneck begins to grind.

"This will not make you stronger. It will make your mistakes less eager to become injuries. Do not confuse those two things."

choices.label: Copy the stabilizing decoction pattern

title: The Cold North Face
body:
The northern edge of the lower grove is a different place. The stone cliff behind it never fully catches the sun, and the cold that settles in the shade over years has changed the soil. The ordinary herbs thin out. In their place, low against the rock, grow unfamiliar stalks — dark green, almost grey, stiff where the frost-vein herbs beside them are supple. The leaves do not curl or droop. They feel cold to the touch even in still air.

A small etched marker set in the cliff face reads: ice-vein herb. Harvest above the root or the sap breaks. Used in breakthrough preparations for qi-stage ceilings.

Your fingers go cold pulling them free. The sap is colourless and smells faintly of deep water.

choices.label: Harvest the stalks carefully — cut above the roots
Study the growth pattern before taking any
Return to the main paths

title: The Method For Mortal Late
body:
The elder examines the draught without opening it.

"Good. The sap held."

He hands it back.

"The method is not complicated, but the timing has no room for hesitation. Sit in full circulation of the Azure Cloud Breathing Method — do not stop the cycle. When you feel the qi moving cleanly, consume the draught. Do not pause afterward. Let the flood come."

He leans forward.

"The ice-vein sap creates a moment of cold stillness before the wolf-core qi disperses. In that moment — that single breath of pressure before the qi scatters — your meridians are loaded past the Mortal Middle ceiling. Push through the centre of that pressure, not around it. If you flinch or try to bleed off the excess, the window closes."

He leans back.

"Go to a quiet room. Do not attempt this tired or half-ready. The draught will not wait for you to compose yourself after you have already drunk it."

choices.label: Return to your room and prepare to attempt it
Ask one more question before leaving

title: Forced Back
body: You stagger away from the mist wolf and retreat to the tablet platform, wounded but alive. The mountain has shown you that cultivation is not only meditation.

choices.label: Return to the mountain gate and begin again

title: The Gate Closes Behind You
body:
The outer gate is not grand from the inside.

From the road it rose like a promise. From the inside it is timber and cloud-iron, and it closes with a sound like a ledger being shut.

Senior Brother Wei walks ahead without hurrying. He has done this before. Around you the other new outer disciples reassemble: some taller now that the trial is finished, some quieter. A merchant's son with his hands folded in his sleeves. The girl who counted her breathing through the cloud mirror. The noble heir who has already found someone to stand behind him.

The outer sect does not announce itself. Stone paths divide between dormitory rows, a medicine garden running in terraced plots down the slope, and a training yard where a senior disciple practices footwork that makes your mortal forms feel like stumbling. Above all of it, the inner sect buildings catch a different quality of light—not brighter, just cleaner—and no outer path leads there.

Senior Brother Wei stops at a notice pillar. His token is older than yours by years.

"I will say what the outer sect requires once," he says. "If you forget, it has been carved into the east dormitory wall for forty years. The sect does not repeat itself without a cost."

choices.label: Stay close to Senior Brother Wei and listen

recordNpcConversation.topic: Listened to his single recitation of the outer sect rules without asking him to repeat anything.

choices.label: Walk alongside Han Yue and compare notes on the exam

recordNpcConversation.topic: Walked beside her on the first day through the outer sect. She had already counted every new disciple and their backgrounds.

choices.label: Watch Liu Zhen introduce himself to a senior disciple
Look up the mountain toward the inner sect path
Follow in silence and observe before speaking

title: Once
body:
Wei speaks without looking down at notes.

"Contribution points run your life here. Gather them from the assignment hall, the medicine garden, arena records, and approved sect tasks. Spend them on scripture access, better dormitory rooms, elder attention, and the privilege of not being assigned laundry rotation.

"Your token opens the outer sect. It does not open the inner sect, the elder pavilion, or the restricted floors of the scripture hall. If a door does not open for your token, you are not meant to be there. There is no 'yet' for the places that simply do not open at all.

"The lower grove is training ground for outer disciples. Do not go past the marked boundary stones after dark. A disciple went past last autumn."

He pauses.

"We logged his token and cleared his room."

No one asks what happened to the disciple. Wei resumes walking as if the question was never available to ask.

choices.label: Follow Senior Brother Wei to the dormitory

title: She Has Been Counting
body:
Han Yue does not look relieved to see you. She looks like someone who has already stopped performing relief and is now working.

"Thirty-one new outer disciples," she says, not quite to you. "Seventeen from families with cultivation connections. Nine from merchant houses. Five commoners."

"You counted us already."

"My brother was in a group of forty-two. He was one of the five commoners. He made it past the cloud mirror and failed the grove trial." She finally looks at you. "I am not going to fail the grove trial."

You walk in silence past the medicine garden. A senior sister is weeding between two rows without looking up.

"Your village prepared you for something," Han Yue says, watching the senior sister's hands. "I could see it in your breathing during the cloud mirror. What was it?"

"People who did not let me waste my foundation."

She nods, once, as if that is exactly the right kind of answer.

choices.label: Ask Han Yue what she has already noticed about the other disciples
Walk with her in quiet toward the dormitory

title: What She Sees
body:
"The merchant's son—Qiao Min—passed because he was too frightened to let himself fail. That kind of fear is a foundation, for now."

She tilts her head toward a knot of three boys in matching blue sashes.

"Those three came together. School friends from a private cultivation house. They will be useful to each other until they are not."

She pauses.

"Liu Zhen—"

"You noticed Liu Zhen."

"Everyone who intends to survive here will notice Liu Zhen," she says. "He came from a family that contributes to the sect every season. He passed the exam, but he did not need to pass it. He needed to be seen passing it cleanly. Those are different things, and he understands the difference."

She glances at you.

"So do you, I think. Or you will."

choices.label: Walk with Han Yue toward the dormitory

title: How Power Works Here
body:
Liu Zhen has already separated himself from the group.

He stands beside a senior outer disciple in a robe slightly better than regulation, speaking with both hands folded in a gesture that looks like humility and is not. The senior disciple is nodding. Liu Zhen says something that makes him laugh.

You are close enough to catch the last piece of it:

"—my family's contribution to the pavilion fund, naturally. Though I hope the sect will judge me by my own effort rather than association."

The senior disciple claps him on the shoulder.

Liu Zhen's eyes move across the senior disciple's shoulder and find you watching. He does not acknowledge you. He turns back to the conversation the way you turn away from furniture.

The dismissal is deliberate enough to be a message. He has already decided what you are worth here. Whether he is right is a different question.

choices.label: Remember what you just saw and walk to the dormitory

title: Assignment Hall Board
body: Three slips remain unclaimed on the lower rows. Most outer disciples collect what they can carry and leave.

choices.label: Gather Frost-Vein Herbs
choices.boardDescription: Collect frost-vein herbs from the lower grove and return them to the hall steward before sundown. A standard outer disciple task.

choices.label: Grove Boundary Watch
choices.boardDescription: Walk the lower grove boundary markers before dark and report anything disturbed or unusual to the hall steward.

choices.label: Medicine Garden Weeding
choices.boardDescription: Clear overgrown weeds from the lower medicine garden terraces. Careful hands preferred over fast ones.

choices.label: Leave the board

title: Grove Boundary, Before Dark
body:
You walk the boundary markers in the fading light. The first two stone discs sit undisturbed. The third is leaning — not fallen, but shifted, as though something pressed against it from outside.

The pine needle bed around it is disturbed in a half-circle, and the disturbance faces inward. Whatever made it came from outside the boundary, not from within.

You mark the location carefully and turn back before the light fails completely.

choices.label: Report the disturbed marker to the hall steward
Report nothing unusual and collect your stones

title: Lower Terrace, Before Noon
body:
The lower terrace is overgrown in patches where a climbing weed has pushed between the root beds. The senior sister who oversees the garden does not give instructions — she watches your hands.

You learn quickly that the weed has two types of root: a shallow runner that pulls clean, and a deeper tap that snaps if you rush it and poisons the bed it came from. Aunt Lin taught you something like this once, and you find yourself slowing down before you mean to.

choices.label: Work carefully, pulling each root clean
Work at a steady pace and finish the section

title: The Path You Cannot Take
body:
The inner sect buildings sit above the cloud line.

From here their details are lost—only shapes where the stone is a different color, where the light does not scatter the same way. A path climbs past the outer sect boundary markers and ends, for you, at a line of small carved discs you are not permitted to cross.

Two inner sect disciples descend past the markers without speaking. Their robes carry the Azure Cloud emblem in silver thread instead of grey. They move through the outer sect like people who have stopped needing to account for the space they occupy.

One of them glances down the slope toward the cluster of new disciples. Something in the look is not unkind, exactly. It is the way you look at a thing that reminds you of yourself before you became something else.

They are gone before you think of anything worth saying.

You stand there long enough to let the path remember your face.

choices.label: Walk to the dormitory with the path in mind

title: Room 47, West Dormitory

image.alt: A quiet wooden outer disciple room with a bed, desk, incense burner, and cloud view
image.caption: Narrow. Plain. The first thing that has been yours alone.

body:
The room is numbered in old brushwork above the lintel. West Dormitory, forty-seventh room. Narrow enough that you can touch both walls with your arms half-extended. A sleeping mat rolled against one side. A small desk with no chair. A window that opens onto the training yard below, where lamps burn low and one senior disciple still runs the same footwork pattern over and over in the dark.

Someone lived here before you. There are four marks scratched into the door frame at chest height—careful, evenly spaced, the kind you make when you want to remember something without writing it down. You do not know what they counted.

Through the wall on your right, someone is cultivating. The rhythm is slow and controlled, not anxious—a person who has been doing this long enough to stop trying. Further down the corridor, someone else has stopped pretending they are not homesick.

The outer sect does not pause for a first night.

choices.label: Sit and attempt the Azure Cloud Breathing Method alone
Lie still and listen to the sect around you
Compose a letter home in your mind
Walk the corridor and see which rooms have a light on
Sleep without cultivating — the body needs rest too

title: The Technique Alone
body:
Without an elder's voice guiding the route, the Azure Cloud Breathing Method is harder to find than you expected.

You sit with your back to the wall and follow the first circulation from memory. The qi moves but not cleanly—it catches somewhere near your chest and disperses before completing the circuit. You reset. Try again. Reset.

On the fourth attempt something settles. Not a breakthrough. Just a recognition: the technique is not asking for effort. It is asking you to stop interfering long enough to let it work.

By the time you stop, the lamp has burned low and your hands are warmer than when you started. Through the wall, the disciple who has been cultivating all evening finally goes still. You did not hear the moment it happened.

choices.label: Sleep and let what settled tonight hold until morning

title: What The Sect Sounds Like
body:
You lie on the mat and let the sect come to you.

Through the east wall: slow, unhurried breathing. Someone who has been cultivating for years and no longer needs to concentrate on the breathing itself.

Through the west wall: nothing. Either an empty room or a very still person.

From the corridor: two senior disciples arguing in low voices about a footwork variation—'the weight drops on the wrong beat, it has always dropped on the wrong beat'—until one of them walks away and the other stands in silence for a long moment before following.

Near midnight, a bell rings once from somewhere above the cloud line. One clean note, no repetition. You do not know what it marks. No one explains it.

Toward dawn the sect grows very quiet. Then, as if a signal passed through the stone itself, it resumes.

choices.label: Rise when the sect rises and face the first full day

title: What You Would Say
body:
You sit at the desk and find that you have no paper.

You sit there anyway.

To Aunt Lin: the medicine garden here runs in terraced rows and the senior sister who tends it has your hands—careful, always checking the root before the leaf. I understand now why you would not let me guess at anything.

To Old Ren: I saw a senior disciple practice footwork in the training yard after dark, alone, running the same pattern until it stopped being practice and became something else. I think you would recognize the discipline. I think you would not be impressed, and that would be the correct response.

To Shrine Keeper Mei: there is a bell above the cloud line that rings once near midnight. I do not know what it is counting. I hope it is not us.

You do not write any of this down. In the morning the letters will not exist and the village will be no closer. But you feel steadier for having known what you would say.

choices.label: Sleep and carry the steadiness into morning

title: Which Rooms Have A Light
body:
The corridor is narrow and smells of pine resin and cold stone. Most doors are dark. A few have thin lamplight at the sill.

One lamp belongs to Qiao Min. You can tell because you can hear him reorganizing something—the sound of a box being opened, repacked, and opened again. A person making order out of objects because he cannot make it out of the situation.

You stop outside Han Yue's door. No light. No sound. Either she is already asleep or she is sitting in the dark deliberately, cultivating or thinking in the way that looks like stillness from outside and is not.

You do not knock on either door. They each have their own first night to get through, and so do you.

At the far end of the corridor, the window opens onto the mountain. The inner sect lights burn above the cloud line like a separate sky.

choices.label: Knock on Qiao Min's door
Return to your room and sleep

title: Too Much Purchased Advice
body:
He opens the door faster than someone who was not already standing at it.

"I was not expecting anyone," he says.

"You were standing at the door."

He steps back to let you in. His room has twice as many objects as yours: a spirit stone lantern still in its wrapping, three manuals fanned open on the desk, a packet of what looks like stamina medicine from a city shop.

"My father's merchant contact gave me seventeen pieces of advice before I left," he says. "I have been trying to decide which eleven are wrong."

"Pick the ones that assume everyone here wants to help you."

He stares at you. Then he picks up the medicine packet and sets it in the far corner of the desk, which is probably the right instinct even if it is not the right reason.

"Were you afraid?" he asks. "During the trial. Were you actually afraid?"

"Yes."

"Good," he says, very quietly. "I thought it was only me."

choices.label: Say goodnight and return to your room

recordNpcConversation.topic: Knocked on his door the first night and told him fear was the right response. He needed to hear it.

title: Your Outer Disciple Room

image.alt: A quiet wooden outer disciple room with a bed, desk, incense burner, and cloud view
image.caption: Narrow walls, plain wood, and a window that opens to clouds.

body: The room is yours. A narrow bed, a low shelf, a window that opens to the mountain air. It is plain, but it is quiet enough to hear your own breath — and in a cultivation sect, that counts for something.

choices.label: Sit and circulate qi — basic morning cultivation
Cultivate on the meditation mat — settle qi more deeply
Study a technique at the desk — read through the forms
Study technique notes under the lamp — deeper focus
Read borrowed notes from the scripture pavilion
Study the herb shelf — trace properties and interactions
Sort the tool rack — keep the Mortal Repair Bundle ready
Prepare medicine at the alchemy workbench
Write in your journal — set down what you have learned
Enter isolation and attempt the Mortal Late breakthrough
Consider upgrading the room
Return to the courtyard

title: Morning Circulation
body: You sit on the bare floor, spine straight, hands loose in your lap. The Azure Cloud Breathing Method carries your qi through its first few cycles. It is a short session — the room has no mat, and the wood is hard — but the breath settles something that talking to other disciples never quite manages.

choices.label: Hold the breath count a little longer before releasing
Finish the cycle and stretch out the stiffness

title: Cultivation on the Mat
body: The meditation mat is a simple thing — woven rush, a faint scent of old incense pressed into the fibers — but it changes the quality of stillness. Your sit bones settle properly. The floor stops complaining. Qi moves more cleanly through the lower channels. An hour here is worth two hours on bare wood.

choices.label: Trace the cloud meridian circuit twice before rising
Rest in stillness after the cycle — let it settle

title: Technique Reading
body: You spread the technique manual on the low shelf and lean close. Without a proper desk the angle is awkward, and the light is poor, but the words are there. You trace the form diagrams with one finger, mouthing the breath counts. What the body cannot perform yet, the mind can rehearse.

choices.label: Focus on the breathing diagrams — trace every count
Skim through the footwork theory — patterns over details

title: Deep Study at the Desk
body: The desk and lamp change the work entirely. A proper surface to spread the manual flat. A steady flame to read by. You can annotate in the margins now — small marks in faint ink — and come back to them. This is how real study works: slow accumulation, one understanding building on the last.

choices.label: Work through the technique's second form in full
Cross-reference two different manuals — look for shared principles

title: Borrowed Scripture Notes
body: The copied pages are dense — scripture pavilion hand, abbreviated and imprecise, but the original intent still bleeds through. Whoever copied these marked certain passages twice. You are not sure whether that means agreement or a warning. Either way, the ideas are alive in a way that the official manual is not.

choices.label: Work through the marked passages slowly
Take notes of your own alongside the copied text

title: Herb Shelf Study
body: The shelf holds a small collection — frost-vein herb, cloud moss, and a few dried samples you have gathered or received. You roll a pinch of frost-vein between your fingers and watch how it bruises: pale green to grey, the sap reluctant. Aunt Lin would call this reading the herb's character. You are beginning to understand what she meant.

choices.label: Compare cloud moss and frost-vein — trace the temperature difference
Study the drying patterns — how a herb changes after harvest

title: Tool Rack Maintenance
body: The tool rack is not impressive at first glance: a few hooks, a narrow tray for rivets, a dry place for thread, and enough order that your Mortal Repair Bundle stops being a lump at the bottom of a pack. That changes more than it should. When every piece has a place, your hands remember where to reach before your thoughts catch up.

choices.label: Repack the bundle for road repairs
Practice quick fixes on old straps and buckles

title: Alchemy Workbench
body:
The workbench is small enough to fit beside the window, but it changes the room's purpose. Dried herbs no longer sit as curiosities on a shelf; they wait in labeled bundles. A clay burner holds steady heat. A shallow stone bowl gives bitter medicine somewhere clean to become useful.

The room smells faintly medicinal now. Not like achievement. Like work you can repeat.

choices.label: Prepare herbs by heat, scent, and patience
Practice keeping residue out of a simple brew

title: Writing in Your Journal
body: You open to a blank page and let the brush sit for a moment. The sect has no requirement that disciples keep records of this kind. But something in you wants an account — not the official one, not what Elder Shen would write about your progress, but a true one. What you actually noticed. What you are still afraid of. What surprised you today.

choices.label: Write about what you have learned so far — take stock honestly
Write down a question you have not yet asked anyone

title: Room Improvements
body: The room can be made more useful one piece at a time. None of the improvements are grand enough to impress an inner disciple, which is exactly why they matter: a mat that makes cultivation easier to repeat, a desk that turns scattered notes into study, shelves and tools that let the room become a working base instead of a place to collapse.

choices.label: Purchase a meditation mat — improves cultivation sessions (5 spirit stones)

messages: The mat changes the room immediately. The floor is still plain wood, but your body no longer has to fight it before cultivation even begins.

choices.label: Purchase a writing desk and lamp — improves study sessions (4 spirit stones)

messages: The desk gives your manuals somewhere to stay open. Study stops feeling stolen from bad posture and dim light.

choices.label: Purchase a small herb shelf — enables herb study (6 spirit stones)

messages: Bundles of cloud moss and frost-vein herb finally have a clean place to dry. The room takes on the quiet bitterness of useful medicine.

choices.label: Install a tool rack — improves field repair work (6 spirit stones, Mortal Repair Bundle)

messages: Hooks, trays, and a dry strip of wall turn the Mortal Repair Bundle into a ready kit instead of a travel lump. Small order becomes real speed.

choices.label: Build an alchemy workbench — unlocks proper medicine prep (10 spirit stones, Medicine Garden trust)

messages: A clay burner, clean stone bowl, and sealed herb drawers make the room suitable for real preparation. Higher medicine recipes no longer have to remain theory.

choices.label: Purchase a bookshelf — enables storing and cross-referencing manuals (12 spirit stones)

messages: The shelf looks empty at first. Then the copied notes, loose pages, and manuals stop being clutter and start becoming a private archive.

choices.label: Leave the room as it is for now

title: Before The Threshold

image.alt: A quiet wooden outer disciple room with a bed, desk, incense burner, and cloud view
image.caption: The room is as quiet as it will ever be.

body:
You have cleared the desk. Drawn the window curtain to a thin line. Placed the Mist-Core Qi Draught where your hand can reach it without looking.

The elder's instructions are clear in your memory. Full Azure Cloud Breathing circulation first — do not stop the cycle. Then drink. Then push into the centre of the pressure when it comes, not away from it.

You sit. You begin the breath count.

The qi moves. The room narrows to this: breath in, breath out, qi through the left meridian path, through the lower channel, back up. Steady. Your hands warm. The draught sits in front of you and does not get smaller no matter how many cycles pass.

You are not afraid of the bottleneck. You are afraid of flinching.

choices.label: Continue the circulation and drink — commit fully to the push
Continue the circulation and drink — the foundation is rough but it will hold
Pull back from the edge — not today

title: Mortal Late
body:
The draught goes down cold.

Then the wolf-core qi arrives — not gradual, not polite — a flood that hits the Mortal Middle ceiling from the inside. The ice-vein sap does exactly what the elder said: it holds the pressure in that single cold stillness, neither dispersing nor pushing forward on its own, waiting for you.

You push.

For three breaths nothing happens. On the fourth, something gives — not a crack, not a snap, more like a locked door that has been leaning the wrong way for years finally yielding to the right angle. Qi rushes into the space that opens.

You stay in the breath count through all of it. You do not flinch.

When the room comes back, your hands are shaking slightly and the incense has burned through. The qi in your body is different — more of it, and heavier, as if it has learned to hold its own weight.

Mortal Late. You did not stumble into it this time. You walked through.

choices.label: Rest and let the qi settle
Return to the courtyard — the sect should see you now

title: Through, At A Cost
body:
The draught goes down and the pressure comes harder than you expected.

The wolf-core qi does not wait for you to find the right angle. It floods, it presses, and the ice-vein stillness lasts only a moment before the pressure starts tearing at your meridians looking for somewhere to go. You push forward anyway — not clean, not elegant, more like forcing a door that was never meant to open this way.

It opens.

You reach Mortal Late, but the passage has left marks. The meridians that bent under the excess qi will need careful work to stabilize. Your impurity count has risen. The foundation cracked slightly at the edges where the pressure was uncontrolled.

The breakthrough counts. The cost counts too.

A senior disciple who passes your door an hour later looks twice at the impurity that has sweated through your outer robe and says nothing.

choices.label: Rest — the body needs to settle after what it just went through
Return to the courtyard

title: The Elder Who Does Not Flinch
body:
Senior Disciple Wei is older than most outer disciples by a decade. He did not enter young, and the sect took him on strength alone — not bloodline, not talent, not backing. He has the quality of someone who has been hit many times and decided, each time, not to stop.

"Iron Body is not a violent method," he says without being asked. "It is a listening method. The qi learns where the body is weak and stands there. When the strike arrives, it finds the qi before it finds you."

He points at your stance without touching you.

"The prerequisite is simple: your body must be strong enough to hold what you are asking it to hold. Too weak a frame and the qi has nowhere to anchor."

choices.label: Ask him to teach the method
You are not ready yet — thank him and leave

title: What The Body Learns
body:
He runs through the basics in a single sitting. The Iron Body Method is short on complexity: root the qi, direct it to the contact surfaces, let the impact travel through instead of stopping at the skin. The theory takes an hour. The practice will take years.

"You will not feel it working at first," he says. "You will feel it working when you notice you should have been hurt more than you were."

At rank 5, the method becomes passive — the qi distribution happens without conscious thought, and your body holds a permanent hardness that persists outside of combat.

choices.label: Return to the blade-scarred arena

title: What The Wind Carries
body:
You sit at the edge of the cloud sea and focus past the visual spectacle. The wind here is not random — it moves in patterns, channels itself through mountain gaps, accelerates around stone edges. Wherever it concentrates, it cuts.

Your mind makes a connection without being asked to. You have been cultivating qi through the Azure Cloud method for long enough that Wind qi is not alien to your meridians. It is just faster, more directional, and eager to be given a shape.

You do not fully understand the technique yet. But the wind has just shown you the form of it.

choices.label: Trace the wind qi pattern through your arm and commit the method to memory
Return to the cloud edge overlook

title: The Edge In The Meridians
body:
You run the wind qi through your arm three times before you trust it. By the third pass it holds a shape you can feel — thin, directional, hungry to release. The Wind Blade is not a complex technique. It is a simple one executed with precision, which is a harder thing to learn than complexity.

The method will sharpen as your Wind affinity deepens. Each point of Wind essence stored in your meridians adds more edge to the strike. For now, it cuts.

choices.label: Return to the cloud edge overlook

title: After The Third Form
body:
You have practised Pine Shadow Step until it no longer requires thought. Your feet know the angles, your weight distributes without instruction, and the technique has stopped being a technique. It has become a habit.

Today something happens after the third form.

There is a half-step — not in the pattern, not taught, not part of the method — where you are momentarily neither in your previous position nor your next one. You simply are not there. And then you are somewhere else, and the air in the space you just occupied takes a moment to fill back in.

You have found the step between steps. Void Step cannot be taught. It can only be discovered.

choices.label: Explore the technique

title: The Space Between
body:
You spend the rest of the afternoon in a forgotten corner of the practice yard, stepping and un-stepping. Each time the half-gap opens, you widen it slightly. Each time you exit into a new position, you try to direct it rather than follow it.

By dusk it is unreliable and exhausting and the most interesting thing you have found in cultivation since the first breathing cycle settled into place. Void Step is not Pine Shadow Step made larger. It treats space less like a fact and more like a suggestion.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: The Token Points North
body:
The trade road looks smaller than you remember. You walked it as a mortal with nothing but a staff and a rumour. Now the same stones feel like scenery.

The token pulls steadily toward a rockfall north of where the bandit fight ended. No footpath leads there. The scrub is dense, the stones are sharp, and someone arranged the approach to feel like a dead end.

You push through the undergrowth. The token grows warm.

choices.label: Push through to the rockfall
Return to the outer sect — come back when ready

title: Beneath The Road
body:
The rockfall is not a rockfall. Three large stones that should have been falling for decades are held in place by a formation thread so faint you nearly missed it. When you press the token against the central stone, the thread shudders and the stones slide aside to reveal a passage carved into the mountain root.

The air that comes out is old. It carries the smell of burnt formation ink and something else — qi that has been circulating in an enclosed space for a very long time.

You light no torch. If there are people down here, light announces you first.

choices.label: Enter the passage
Study the entrance formation before going in
Return to the outer sect — this can wait

title: The First Chamber
body:
The passage opens into a wide chamber lit by formation stones set at head height. The light is cold and blue — the colour of qi run through something it should not pass through.

Three tunnels branch from the far wall. Left: the faint sound of movement and the smell of something old and wrong. Centre: silence so complete it feels maintained. Right: a rhythmic pulse low enough to feel through the soles of your feet.

The tattered notes in your pack suddenly make more sense. This is the formation the corrupt disciple was writing about.

choices.label: Take the left tunnel — meet whatever is moving directly
Take the centre tunnel — move without being seen
Trace the formation rhythm using your notes from the corrupt disciple
Attune your spiritual sense to the formation rhythm

title: Teeth In The Dark
body:
The left tunnel smells of something dead and something that is not. Formation torches dot the walls at long intervals, but between them the dark is total. Things move in the gaps — not randomly, but in a patrol pattern maintained by something that does not sleep or tire.

A bone hound assembles itself from the shadow ahead, drawing from pieces that do not all belong together. It does not growl. It simply commits.

choices.label: Face the bone hound
Back into the antechamber — choose a different route

title: Bone Ward
body: The bone hound closes before you finish your breath. The pieces that make it up are from different creatures — the join points visible as it moves. Whatever qi is holding this together is old and has learned the shape of violence.

choices.label: Return to the combat passage

title: Through The Bone Ward
body:
The hound collapses in pieces. You step over what remains and press deeper. The combat passage opens into a wider space — the walls here are carved with thread-work patterns that match the token at your belt exactly.

Someone spent years in this place building toward something. A supply alcove holds dried rations, a pack of binding herbs, and three sealed spirit stones that never made it out.

choices.label: Continue to the inner ward

title: Between Moments
body:
The centre tunnel is silent in the way only maintained silence can be. Two mist shades drift in overlapping circuits through the passage — their attention cycles in regular patterns, leaving gaps. Each gap is exactly long enough if you do not hesitate.

Pine Shadow Step was made for corridors like this. Your feet already understand the interval.

choices.label: Move through the circuit gap
Challenge the mist shades directly
Back into the antechamber — choose a different route

title: Facing The Cold
body:
Both shades turn toward you at the same moment. They knew you were there. Whatever circuit they were running, it was also a net.

You are committed now.

choices.label: Return to the stealth passage

title: Unseen Through The Ward
body:
The shades drift past on either side, close enough that the cold radiates off them. Your footwork holds. Your breathing holds. You find the far end of the passage and step through before the circuit completes.

Your heart is running faster than the technique wants it to, but you are through.

choices.label: Continue to the inner ward

title: The Space The Shades Did Not Guard
body:
Void Step carries you through a gap in the circuit that Pine Shadow Step would have navigated around. You pass through it — and on the other side, in a niche the shades clearly cannot perceive, you find a sealed cache.

Inside: a page in a hand you have seen before on the tattered sect notes. The writing is cleaner here, less hurried. It names the hollow, names the order, and lists three secondary sites. Someone was keeping records of their own operation.

choices.label: Take the record and continue to the inner ward

title: The Stone That Remembers
body:
The right tunnel's heartbeat is a formation timer — the kind used to cycle qi renewal through a sealed space. The patterns on the wall are not decoration. They are records.

The formation was not built to guard something. It was built to drain something — a slow siphon anchored below the Azure Cloud mountain root, drawing qi from the sect's base formation one thread at a time. Each thread too thin to detect. The total, compounding over years, is considerable.

Someone patient built this. Someone who could afford to wait.

choices.label: Follow the formation thread toward its anchor point

title: The Inner Ward
body:
The passages converge in a chamber the size of a small courtyard. The formation here is active — threads of qi move through carved channels in the floor, gathering on a raised dais at the far end. The dais holds a structure like a web of condensed black qi, dense enough to be visible in the air.

A figure stands at the centre of it. Robed. Motionless. The black thread tattoos move across his hands even at rest.

He heard you coming anyway.

choices.label: Study the formation before approaching
Approach the figure directly

title: What The Threads Take
body:
The formation is a masterwork of patience. Black qi threads run through the carved floor channels, gathering at the dais and cycling back to the outer wall. You trace the flow — it goes deeper than the room, through the mountain root, in the direction of the Azure Cloud Sect.

Over years, maybe decades, this formation has been pulling minute amounts from the sect's base formation. Not enough to trigger detection. Each thread is a whisper, but whispers compound. The structure on the dais is filled with what was taken.

The person standing at its centre is not guarding the formation. He is part of it.

choices.label: You have seen enough. Face him.

title: The Man At The Centre
body:
He turns before you speak. The calculation in his expression is quiet and efficient.

"Outer disciple. You followed the token north. That means one of my agents failed somewhere along the road."

He does not move from the dais.

"Leave now and I will not report you to the sect as a trespasser. The formation cannot be dismantled from this end — the siphon threads are anchored on the sect side, not here. There is nothing for you to accomplish except being hurt."

He believes this. That is either honesty or a very specific kind of trap.

choices.label: You are not leaving. The formation ends today.
Ask him who he answers to

title: The Order Behind The Thread
body:
He considers the question long enough that you understand he has considered answering it before.

"I answer to an order older than this sect's current elders. We collect what spills from the great formations — what would otherwise dissipate unacknowledged. A river does not mourn the water it never touched."

He pauses.

"We do not oppose the Azure Cloud Sect specifically. We existed before it and will exist after. You, personally, are not important to this operation."

The rationalisation is smooth. Practiced. And it is not actually an answer to your question.

choices.label: The answer is no.

title: Black Thread Method, Unrestrained
body:
He does not move dramatically. He simply stops choosing not to fight.

The formation web behind him flares — the collected qi floods into the thread tattoos and they begin moving with purpose. Whatever ceiling he was maintaining on his method, he has removed it. The temperature in the chamber drops several degrees.

choices.label: Hold your ground

title: Thread Against Foundation
body: The black-thread method operates differently from anything in the Azure Cloud Sect curriculum. It does not push. It pulls — drawing on the ambient formation qi in the chamber, directing it through the thread tattoos as a conductor.

choices.label: Return to the formation chamber

title: After The Formation Breaks
body:
He goes down. The formation web flickers and begins to unravel — without the cultist to maintain it, the siphon loses coherence and the collected qi disperses into the chamber walls. You can feel it against your meridians: months or years of Azure Cloud qi returning to circulation at once.

The chamber is quiet. You are breathing harder than the fight warranted. The method he used pressed against something in your qi flow that will need a day to settle.

Search this place before you leave.

choices.label: Search the formation chamber

title: What The Hollow Kept
body:
The cultist's effects are meticulous: a sealed codex detailing the black-thread order's philosophy, active operations, and three secondary siphon sites. A sealed technique scroll carrying qi unlike anything in the outer sect library — sharp, fast, running counter to the calm cloud methods you were taught. A pale root the length of a forearm growing from a fissure in the wall, absorbing formation overflow for what appears to be years.

Each item represents something the sect's elders will want to see. Each also represents something worth keeping.

choices.label: Study the technique scroll here while the formation qi still permeates the air
Secure everything and study the scroll at the sect

title: A Different Current
body:
The scroll opens to a method unlike anything in the Azure Cloud Sect's teaching. Where cloud qi is slow and accumulative, this technique is immediate — it reads the ambient electrical charge in the air and concentrates it into a discharged strike. The instruction is terse and assumes a reader who already cultivates. Your meridians, still warm from the formation qi dispersal, absorb the pattern quickly.

By the time you roll the scroll closed, the technique has settled into place. It will sharpen with practice.

choices.label: Leave the hollow

title: Back Into The Mountain Air
body:
The passage out is shorter than the passage in. The formation seal does not close behind you — without its anchor, the stones have lost their mechanism. The hollow is open now to whoever finds it next.

You stand in the scrub north of the broken trade road with new weight in your pack and a different weight in your meridians. The token no longer hums. Whatever purpose it was built for has ended.

choices.label: Study the lightning method scroll here in the quiet
Return to the outer sect

title: Driven From The Hollow
body:
The method overwhelms you. You do not remember making the decision to retreat — only that the passage is behind you and then the cold mountain air is around you and you are moving through the scrub with nothing but the instinct to put distance between yourself and what is below.

He did not pursue. You were not important enough for that.

The hollow is still there. So is he. Come back with more.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect and recover

title: The Lower Gate
body:
The outer sect path drops steeply at its southern end, where the mountain road begins in earnest. A lone disciple guard at the lower gate marks your token with a brush and says nothing — disciples come and go, and your token is in order.

Below the gate, the mountain road winds south through old pine forest. Iron Bridge Town sits at the base of the range, a full day's walk in clear weather. The road is maintained by merchant guilds, not the sect.

choices.label: Head south toward Iron Bridge Town
Return to the outer sect paths

title: Mountain Pass
body:
The road narrows at the first pass, where a landslide left permanent rubble along the right shoulder. From the rubble, three figures step onto the road. They wear no sect markings — road men, spread and watching. The one in front carries a notched saber. The others hold staves.

They are watching where your hands are.

choices.label: Draw and close the distance before they can spread further
Leave the road and move silently through the uphill brush
Hold your ground and let your cultivation base speak for itself
Turn back — the road can wait

title: Saber And Road Dust
body: The saber man moves first — low and fast, the kind of opening that has dropped unprepared cultivators before. You close the gap before his men can flank.

opening: Gravel shifts underfoot as {enemy} raises his saber and steps into range.
The pass narrows as {enemy} signals his men and charges with the blade low.
strike: You drive through his guard and hit {enemy} for {damage} damage.
Dust kicks up from the rubble as your strike lands on {enemy} for {damage} damage.
You check the saber with your forearm and answer it with {damage} damage.
focus: You channel qi into your arms and cut through his guard for {damage} damage.
A focused breath tightens the strike — {enemy} takes {damage} damage.
Qi floods your limbs as you press forward, dealing {damage} damage to {enemy}.
enemyAttack: {enemy} drives a hard saber cut that connects for {damage} damage.
The notched blade catches your shoulder for {damage} damage.
{enemy} sidesteps and slashes across your guard for {damage} damage.
victory: {enemy} drops the saber and backs into the rubble, breathing hard.
The saber man goes down in the dust. His men scatter into the treeline.
defeat: {enemy} drives you back toward the sect gate, every step costing blood.

title: South Of The Pass
body:
The road opens below the pass. Pine forest gives way to lower valley growth — birch, cultivated terraces, and patches of farmland following the stream south.

By the time the afternoon light drops toward the western ridge, Iron Bridge Town is visible at the valley floor: tile roofs, a stone bridge, and cooking smoke rising through the cool air.

choices.label: Enter Iron Bridge Town

title: Driven Back
body:
They were better prepared than they looked. You made it back through the pass with most of your blood still in you — barely — and turned before they pressed the advantage.

The mountain road south is closed until you are stronger or more careful. Your wounds are not fatal. They are honest.

choices.label: Retreat to the outer sect

title: Iron Bridge Town
body:
The town sits at the base of the mountain road where three trade routes converge. The iron bridge that gave it its name still stands — wide enough for two carts abreast, its old rivets stained black from a hundred wet seasons.

The main road splits inside the gate. Left leads to the market row. Right heads toward the craftsman quarter. Straight on is the River Lantern Inn, a painted lantern swinging over its door. There are cultivators among the crowd — not many, but more than a mortal village would ever see. Most wear no sect markings.

choices.label: Walk the market row
Find the blacksmith in the craftsman quarter
Step into the River Lantern Inn
Check the notice board outside the inn
Head back north toward the sect

title: Market Row
body: The market row is longer than it looks from the gate. Stalls sell preserved spirit herbs, sealed core containers, road tools, and goods that need no explanation. A vendor at one end runs the elixir counter — clean stock, standard prices, no hagglingaccepted. A second stall handles materials: raw herbs, low-grade components, and a few things collected from the surrounding hills.

choices.label: Browse the elixir counter
Check the materials stall
Return to the main street

title: Elixir Counter
body: The vendor keeps a ledger propped open beside the bottles. The stock is clean — no obvious impurity trace in the outer layer, which is more than the village market ever offered. She does not negotiate on price.

choices.label: Buy a standard qi pill (3 spirit stones)
Buy a road healing draught (2 spirit stones)
Return to the market row

title: Materials Vendor
body: Crates of dried herbs and sealed components line the table. The vendor charges what the posted sign says — no more, no less — with the comfortable authority of someone who has not needed to haggle in years.

choices.label: Buy a frost-vein herb bundle (1 spirit stone)
Buy a cooling herb salve (1 spirit stone)
Return to the market row

title: Luo's Iron Works
body:
The smithy smells of coal and cooling metal. A compact man in a work apron — forearms scarred from years of hot spark work, both of them — looks up from a half-finished fitting and gives you a quick reading. He is not looking at your robe. He is looking at your hands.

He taps the anvil twice with his knuckle before speaking. "Outer sect?" Not unfriendly. Just efficient.

choices.label: Buy the spirit-grade shortsword on the rack (5 spirit stones)
Buy a crude leather vest (3 spirit stones)
Ask about the bounty posting on the notice board

recordNpcConversation.topic: boar_bounty

choices.label: Offer to sort salvage and learn how Luo binds rough materials
Ask Luo to teach you practical field crafting
Show Luo your beast materials and ask what they can become
Ask about advanced staff and spear work
Deliver proof — the spirit boar is dead

messages: Luo takes the hide, turns it over once, and sets it on the counter. He does not say anything about the quality. He counts three spirit stones from a drawer and places them in front of you. "East terraces will hold this season," he says. That is all the acknowledgment you are going to get.

choices.label: Return to the main street

title: Work Beside The Anvil
body:
Luo points you toward a crate of snapped buckles, bent nails, bone chips, and copper scrap.

"Sort what can hold pressure from what only looks useful," he says. "Half of craft is not lying to yourself about materials. Other half is not lying to the person who has to wear the result."

By the time the crate is clean, your fingers smell of iron and old road dust. Luo gives one short nod.

choices.label: Return to the counter

title: Patterns For Road Gear
body:
Luo sketches three patterns on the back of an old receipt: binding strips, a scale-backed blade, and a vest reinforcement that puts ugly practicality before elegance.

"These are not sect secrets," he says. "They are things people survive because someone bothered to make them properly."

choices.label: Memorize the practical patterns

title: Shell, Hide, And Blood
body:
Luo turns the stone crab shell under the light, then presses a thumb against the beast hide until the hide creaks.

"Good shell wants to stay shell. Good hide wants to stay animal. If you force them together, they split. If you give them a reason to agree, they save ribs."

He marks two heavier patterns and a sword-hardening method beside them.

choices.label: Study the heavier beast-material patterns

title: Reach And Restraint
body:
Luo weighs the cloud stag antler, then looks at your stance.

"People think long weapons are for hitting first. Wrong. They are for deciding who gets close enough to matter. If you want one, build it like the shaft is a promise you intend to keep."

He shows you how antler joins to bamboo, then how binding strips can turn a reinforced staff into a safer weapon.

choices.label: Commit the staff and spear patterns to memory

title: The Boar At The East Terrace
body:
Luo leans on the counter and considers you for a moment.

"Spirit boar," he says. "Bigger than the grove variety. Been tearing through the east crop terraces for three weeks. Town guard can't touch it — wrong tools and wrong nerves. The posting has an Azure Cloud seal, which means the sect placed the bounty but didn't send anyone to collect."

He taps the anvil again. "Three spirit stones, on proof. You'll know what proof means after you've been there."

choices.label: Head to the eastern terraces
Return to the smithy

title: Eastern Crop Terraces
body:
The terraces step down the eastern hillside in narrow strips, each one generations of farming work. The damage is systematic — not a beast marking territory, but one working through the harvest row by row.

At the lowest terrace, you find it. The spirit boar is massive for its kind, layered with ground qi absorbed over years. Its tusks are stained dark. It turns to face you without hesitation.

choices.label: Engage the spirit boar
Pull back — this beast is larger than expected

title: Eastern Terraces
body: The spirit boar charges down the terrace slope with the force of something that has been destroying things for weeks without resistance.

opening: {enemy} charges from the lower terrace, tusks forward, moving faster than a beast its size should.
Ground qi radiates off {enemy}'s hide as it closes the distance with lowered head.
strike: You sidestep the charge and land a blow on {enemy}'s flank for {damage} damage.
Your strike catches {enemy} at the shoulder for {damage} damage.
You drive into the opening after the charge and deal {damage} damage.
focus: You pull qi into the strike and drive through the thick hide for {damage} damage.
A focused blow connects with the bone beneath the qi-dense hide for {damage} damage.
